Some of its notable moves include Ion Deluge (level 1), Shock Wave (level 11), Flame Charge (level 18), Pursuit (level 22), Spark (level 25), Stomp (level 31). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Roggenrola
text: Roggenrola is a Rock type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Mantle Pokémon. Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel. They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core. The hexagonal-shaped hole is its ear. It has a tendency to walk in the direction the sound is coming from. Born deep within the ground, it compares itself with similar species, such as Geodude and Carbink, to settle which has the hardest body. The hexagonal cavity is its ear. It walks in the direction of sounds it hears, but if the sounds cease, it panics and topples over. The hexagonal hole is its ear. Deep in that ear is an energy core, so if you stick your hand in there, Roggenrola will be very angry! Its base stats are 55 HP, 75 Attack, 85 Defense, 25 Special Attack, 25 Special Defense, 15 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 280. It has the abilities Sturdy and Weak Armor, with the hidden ability Sand Force. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 18.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Headbutt (level 10), Rock Blast (level 14), Mud Slap (level 17), Iron Defense (level 20), Smack Down (level 23), Rock Slide (level 27). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Boldore
text: Boldore is a Rock type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Ore Pokémon. It evolves from Roggenrola. When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves. Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals. When it is healthy, its core sticks out. Always facing the same way, it swiftly moves front to back and left to right. Its orange crystal is a mass of energy. Just one crystal fragment would provide enough fuel for a hundred dump trucks. It explores caves in search of underground water. It’s not comfortable around water, so this Pokémon takes great care in lapping it up. The energy overflowing from its body has turned into orange crystals that are hard enough to smash diamonds. Its base stats are 70 HP, 105 Attack, 105 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 20 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 390. It has the abilities Sturdy and Weak Armor, with the hidden ability Sand Force. It stands 0.9 meters tall and weighs 102.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Rock Blast (level 14), Mud Slap (level 17), Iron Defense (level 20), Smack Down (level 23), Power Gem (level 25), Rock Slide (level 30). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Gigalith
text: Gigalith is a Rock type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Compressed Pokémon. It evolves from Boldore. Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain. The solar energy absorbed by its body’s orange crystals is magnified internally and fired from its mouth. The solar rays it absorbs are processed in its energy core and fired as a ball of light. The blasts of energy it makes from sunbeams have terrifying power. However, it’s not able to fire its blasts at night or on rainy days. Known for its hefty horsepower, this Pokémon is a popular partner for construction workers. When it fires off energy with all its might, the power creates countless fissures on its body. Its base stats are 85 HP, 135 Attack, 130 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 25 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 515. It has the abilities Sturdy and Sand Stream, with the hidden ability Sand Force. It stands 1.7 meters tall and weighs 260.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Rock Blast (level 14), Mud Slap (level 17), Iron Defense (level 20), Smack Down (level 23), Power Gem (level 25), Rock Slide (level 30). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Woobat
text: Woobat is a Psychic and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Bat Pokémon. Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings. Suction from its nostrils enables it to stick to cave walls during sleep. It leaves a heart-shaped mark behind. The heart-shaped mark left on a body after a Woobat has been attached to it is said to bring good fortune. While inside a cave, if you look up and see lots of heart-shaped marks lining the walls, it’s evidence that Woobat live there. It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon. Its base stats are 65 HP, 45 Attack, 43 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 43 Special Defense, 72 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 323. It has the abilities Unaware and Klutz, with the hidden ability Simple. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 2.1 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Imprison (level 19), Air Cutter (level 21), Attract (level 25), Amnesia (level 29), Calm Mind (level 29), Air Slash (level 32). It belongs to the Ground and Flying egg groups.

title: Swoobat
text: Swoobat is a Psychic and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Courting Pokémon. It evolves from Woobat. It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks. Anyone who comes into contact with the ultrasonic waves emitted by a courting male experiences a positive mood shift. It shakes its tail vigorously when it emits ultrasonic waves strong enough to reduce concrete to rubble. Emitting powerful sound waves tires it out. Afterward, it won’t be able to fly for a little while. The auspicious shape of this Pokémon’s nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck. Its base stats are 67 HP, 57 Attack, 55 Defense, 77 Special Attack, 55 Special Defense, 114 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 425. It has the abilities Unaware and Klutz, with the hidden ability Simple. It stands 0.9 meters tall and weighs 10.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Imprison (level 19), Air Cutter (level 21), Attract (level 25), Amnesia (level 29), Calm Mind (level 29), Air Slash (level 32). It belongs to the Ground and Flying egg groups.

title: Drilbur
text: Drilbur is a Ground type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Mole Pokémon. It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It could give a car running aboveground a good race. It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting both claws together and rotating at high speed. By spinning its body, it can dig straight through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It brings its claws together and whirls around at high speed before rushing toward its prey. It’s a digger, using its claws to burrow through the ground. It causes damage to vegetable crops, so many farmers have little love for it. Its base stats are 60 HP, 85 Attack, 40 Defense, 30 Special Attack, 45 Special Defense, 68 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 328. It has the abilities Sand Rush and Sand Force, with the hidden ability Mold Breaker. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 8.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Dig (level 19), Hone Claws (level 22), Crush Claw (level 24), Slash (level 26), Rock Slide (level 29), Earthquake (level 33). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Excadrill
text: Excadrill is a Ground and Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Subterrene Pokémon. It evolves from Drilbur. It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates. More than 300 feet below the surface, they build mazelike nests. Their activity can be destructive to subway tunnels. Forming a drill with its steel claws and head, it can bore through a steel plate, no matter how thick it is. It’s not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill’s rampant digging. Known as the Drill King, this Pokémon can tunnel through the terrain at speeds of over 90 mph. Its base stats are 110 HP, 135 Attack, 60 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 65 Special Defense, 88 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 508. It has the abilities Sand Rush and Sand Force, with the hidden ability Mold Breaker. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 40.4 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Hone Claws (level 22), Crush Claw (level 24), Slash (level 26), Rock Slide (level 29), Horn Drill (level 31), Earthquake (level 36). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Audino
text: Audino is a Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Hearing Pokémon. It touches others with the feelers on its ears, using the sound of their heartbeats to tell how they are feeling. Its auditory sense is astounding. It has a radarlike ability to understand its surroundings through slight sounds. Using the feelers on its ears, it can tell how someone is feeling or when an egg might hatch. Audino’s sense of hearing is superb. Not even a pebble rolling along over a mile away will escape Audino’s ears. This Pokémon has a kind heart. By touching with its feelers, Audino can gauge other creatures’ feelings and physical conditions. Its base stats are 103 HP, 60 Attack, 86 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 86 Special Defense, 50 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 445. It has the abilities Healer and Regenerator, with the hidden ability Klutz. It stands 1.1 meters tall and weighs 31.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Secret Power (level 20), Zen Headbutt (level 20), Life Dew (level 24), Entrainment (level 25), Take Down (level 30), Heal Pulse (level 35). It belongs to the Fairy egg groups.

title: Timburr
text: Timburr is a Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Muscular Pokémon. It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty. These Pokémon appear at building sites and help out with construction. They always carry squared logs. Always carrying squared logs, they help out with construction. As they grow, they carry bigger logs. It loves helping out with construction projects. It loves it so much that if rain causes work to halt, it swings its log around and throws a tantrum. Timburr that have started carrying logs that are about three times their size are nearly ready to evolve. Its base stats are 75 HP, 80 Attack, 55 Defense, 25 Special Attack, 35 Special Defense, 35 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 305. It has the abilities Guts and Sheer Force, with the hidden ability Iron Fist. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 12.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slam (level 24), Chip Away (level 24), Bulk Up (level 28), Rock Slide (level 31), Dynamic Punch (level 34), Scary Face (level 37). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Gurdurr
text: Gurdurr is a Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Muscular Pokémon. It evolves from Timburr. This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch. They strengthen their bodies by carrying steel beams. They show off their big muscles to their friends. With strengthened bodies, they skillfully wield steel beams to take down buildings. It shows off its muscles to Machoke and other Gurdurr. If it fails to measure up to the other Pokémon, it lies low for a little while. Gurdurr excels at demolition—construction is not its forte. In any case, there’s skill in the way this Pokémon wields its metal beam. Its base stats are 85 HP, 105 Attack, 85 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 40 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 405. It has the abilities Guts and Sheer Force, with the hidden ability Iron Fist. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 40.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slam (level 24), Chip Away (level 24), Bulk Up (level 29), Rock Slide (level 33), Dynamic Punch (level 37), Scary Face (level 41). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Conkeldurr
text: Conkeldurr is a Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Muscular Pokémon. It evolves from Gurdurr. It is thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago. They use concrete pillars as walking canes. They know moves that enable them to swing the pillars freely in battle. Rather than rely on force, they master moves that utilize the centrifugal force of spinning concrete. Concrete mixed by Conkeldurr is much more durable than normal concrete, even when the compositions of the two materials are the same. When going all out, this Pokémon throws aside its concrete pillars and leaps at opponents to pummel them with its fists. Its base stats are 105 HP, 140 Attack, 95 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 65 Special Defense, 45 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 505. It has the abilities Guts and Sheer Force, with the hidden ability Iron Fist. It stands 1.4 meters tall and weighs 87.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slam (level 24), Chip Away (level 24), Bulk Up (level 29), Rock Slide (level 33), Dynamic Punch (level 37), Scary Face (level 41). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Tympole
text: Tympole is a Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Tadpole Pokémon. They warn others of danger by vibrating their cheeks to create a high-pitched sound. By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk. By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans and warns others of danger. Graceful ripples running across the water’s surface are a sure sign that Tympole are singing in high-pitched voices below. It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can’t hear its cries of warning. Its base stats are 50 HP, 50 Attack, 40 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 64 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 294. It has the abilities Swift Swim and Hydration, with the hidden ability Water Absorb. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 4.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bubble Beam (level 12), Mud Shot (level 16), Aqua Ring (level 20), Uproar (level 23), Muddy Water (level 27), Rain Dance (level 31). It belongs to the Water1 egg groups.

title: Palpitoad
text: Palpitoad is a Water and Ground type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Vibration Pokémon. It evolves from Tympole. When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land. It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to capture prey. It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to immobilize its opponents. It weakens its prey with sound waves intense enough to cause headaches, then entangles them with its sticky tongue. On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds. Its base stats are 75 HP, 65 Attack, 55 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 55 Special Defense, 69 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 384. It has the abilities Swift Swim and Hydration, with the hidden ability Water Absorb. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 17.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bubble Beam (level 12), Mud Shot (level 16), Aqua Ring (level 20), Uproar (level 23), Muddy Water (level 28), Rain Dance (level 33). It belongs to the Water1 egg groups.

title: Seismitoad
text: Seismitoad is a Water and Ground type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Vibration Pokémon. It evolves from Palpitoad. They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents. It increases the power of its punches by vibrating the bumps on its fists. It can turn a boulder to rubble with one punch. By putting power into its bumps, it creates vibrations and increases the power of its punches. The vibrating of the bumps all over its body causes earthquake-like tremors. Seismitoad and Croagunk are similar species. This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages. Its base stats are 105 HP, 95 Attack, 75 Defense, 85 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 74 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 509. It has the abilities Swift Swim and Poison Touch, with the hidden ability Water Absorb. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 62.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Aqua Ring (level 20), Uproar (level 23), Muddy Water (level 28), Rain Dance (level 33), Acid (level 36), Flail (level 39). It belongs to the Water1 egg groups.

title: Throh
text: Throh is a Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Judo Pokémon. When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts. When they encounter foes bigger than themselves, they try to throw them. They always travel in packs of five. When it encounters a foe bigger than itself, it wants to throw it. It changes belts as it gets stronger. It performs throwing moves with first-rate skill. Over the course of many battles, Throh’s belt grows darker as it absorbs its wearer’s sweat. They train in groups of five. Any member that can’t keep up will discard its belt and leave the group. Its base stats are 120 HP, 100 Attack, 85 Defense, 30 Special Attack, 85 Special Defense, 45 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 465. It has the abilities Guts and Inner Focus, with the hidden ability Mold Breaker. It stands 1.3 meters tall and weighs 55.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Vital Throw (level 17), Revenge (level 21), Storm Throw (level 25), Body Slam (level 29), Bulk Up (level 33), Circle Throw (level 37). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Sawk
text: Sawk is a Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Karate Pokémon. The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train. Tying their belts gets them pumped and makes their punches more destructive. Disturbing their training angers them. Desiring the strongest karate chop, they seclude themselves in mountains and train without sleeping. If you see a Sawk training in the mountains in its single-minded pursuit of strength, it’s best to quietly pass by. The karate chops of a Sawk that’s trained itself to the limit can cleave the ocean itself. Its base stats are 75 HP, 125 Attack, 75 Defense, 30 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 85 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 465. It has the abilities Sturdy and Inner Focus, with the hidden ability Mold Breaker. It stands 1.4 meters tall and weighs 51.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Low Sweep (level 17), Counter (level 21), Karate Chop (level 25), Brick Break (level 29), Bulk Up (level 33), Retaliate (level 37). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Sewaddle
text: Sewaddle is a Bug and Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Sewing Pokémon. Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping. This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth. Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers. Its base stats are 45 HP, 53 Attack, 70 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 42 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 310. It has the abilities Swarm and Chlorophyll, with the hidden ability Overcoat. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 2.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Tackle (level 1), String Shot (level 1), Bug Bite (level 8), Razor Leaf (level 15), Struggle Bug (level 22), Endure (level 29). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Swadloon
text: Swadloon is a Bug and Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Leaf-Wrapped Pokémon. It evolves from Sewaddle. Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life. It protects itself from the cold by wrapping up in leaves. It stays on the move, eating leaves in forests. Preferring dark, damp places, it spends the entire day eating fallen leaves that lie around it. Its base stats are 55 HP, 63 Attack, 90 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 42 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 380. It has the abilities Leaf Guard and Chlorophyll, with the hidden ability Overcoat. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 7.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Razor Leaf (level 1), String Shot (level 1), Grass Whistle (level 1), Bug Bite (level 1), Protect (level 20), Struggle Bug (level 22). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Leavanny
text: Leavanny is a Bug and Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Nurturing Pokémon. It evolves from Swadloon. Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk. It keeps its eggs warm with heat from fermenting leaves. It also uses leaves to make warm wrappings for Sewaddle. Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves by using the sticky silk secreted from its mouth. Its base stats are 75 HP, 103 Attack, 80 Defense, 70 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 92 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 500. It has the abilities Swarm and Chlorophyll, with the hidden ability Overcoat. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 20.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bug Bite (level 1), Struggle Bug (level 22), Slash (level 29), Helping Hand (level 32), Fell Stinger (level 34), Leaf Blade (level 36). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Venipede
text: Venipede is a Bug and Poison type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Centipede Pokémon. Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it. It discovers what is going on around it by using the feelers on its head and tail. It is brutally aggressive. Using the feelers on its head and tail, it picks up vibrations in the air to determine its prey’s location and state. Venipede and Sizzlipede are similar species, but when the two meet, a huge fight ensues. Its fangs are highly venomous. If this Pokémon finds prey it thinks it can eat, it leaps for them without any thought of how things might turn out. Its base stats are 30 HP, 45 Attack, 59 Defense, 30 Special Attack, 39 Special Defense, 57 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 260. It has the abilities Poison Point and Swarm, with the hidden ability Speed Boost. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 5.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Poison Tail (level 19), Bug Bite (level 22), Venoshock (level 26), Take Down (level 28), Agility (level 29), Steamroller (level 33). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Whirlipede
text: Whirlipede is a Bug and Poison type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Curlipede Pokémon. It evolves from Venipede. Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies. It is usually motionless, but when attacked, it rotates at high speed and then crashes into its opponent. Storing energy for evolution, it sits. But, when predators approach, it moves to stab them with poison spikes. This Pokémon spins itself rapidly and charges into its opponents. Its top speed is just over 60 mph. Whirlipede protects itself with a sturdy shell and poisonous spikes while it stores up the energy it’ll need for evolution. Its base stats are 40 HP, 55 Attack, 99 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 79 Special Defense, 47 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 360. It has the abilities Poison Point and Swarm, with the hidden ability Speed Boost. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 58.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Iron Defense (level 22), Bug Bite (level 23), Venoshock (level 28), Take Down (level 32), Agility (level 32), Steamroller (level 37). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Scolipede
text: Scolipede is a Bug and Poison type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Megapede Pokémon. It evolves from Whirlipede. With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails. Highly aggressive, it uses the claws near its neck to dig into its opponents and poison them. It clasps its prey with the claws on its neck until it stops moving. Then it finishes it off with deadly poison. Scolipede latches on to its prey with the claws on its neck before slamming them into the ground and jabbing them with its claws’ toxic spikes. Scolipede engage in fierce territorial battles with Centiskorch. At the end of one of these battles, the victor makes a meal of the loser. Its base stats are 60 HP, 100 Attack, 89 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 69 Special Defense, 112 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 485. It has the abilities Poison Point and Swarm, with the hidden ability Speed Boost. It stands 2.5 meters tall and weighs 200.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bug Bite (level 23), Venoshock (level 28), Baton Pass (level 30), Agility (level 33), Take Down (level 34), Steamroller (level 39). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Cottonee
text: Cottonee is a Grass and Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Cotton Puff Pokémon. When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker. They go wherever the wind takes them. On rainy days, their bodies are heavier, so they take shelter beneath big trees. Perhaps because they feel more at ease in a group, they stick to others they find. They end up looking like a cloud. When it finds others of its kind, they all stick together. When enough of them have collected, the mass resembles a cumulonimbus cloud. Pillows and beds stuffed with cotton exhaled by Cottonee are soft and puffy, light and airy— altogether top quality. To protect itself, it shoots cotton from its body. When it gets wet in the rain, its cotton grows moist and heavy, and it can’t move as well. Its base stats are 40 HP, 27 Attack, 60 Defense, 37 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 66 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 280. It has the abilities Prankster and Infiltrator, with the hidden ability Chlorophyll. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 0.6 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Razor Leaf (level 19), Poison Powder (level 22), Giga Drain (level 26), Charm (level 28), Helping Hand (level 31), Energy Ball (level 35). It belongs to the Plant and Fairy egg groups.

title: Whimsicott
text: Whimsicott is a Grass and Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Windveiled Pokémon. It evolves from Cottonee. Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind. Riding whirlwinds, they appear. These Pokémon sneak through gaps into houses and cause all sorts of mischief. They appear along with whirlwinds. They pull pranks, such as moving furniture and leaving balls of cotton in homes. It rides on the wind and slips into people’s homes. After it has turned a room into a cotton- filled mess, it giggles to itself and takes off. This Pokémon appears, riding upon the wind. But if the wind gusts up, it’ll blow the cotton on this Pokémon’s head clean off. This nuisance sneaks into people’s homes, where it hides important things and scatters cotton all over the place. Its base stats are 60 HP, 67 Attack, 85 Defense, 77 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 116 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 480. It has the abilities Prankster and Infiltrator, with the hidden ability Chlorophyll. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 6.6 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Memento (level 1), Helping Hand (level 1), Endeavor (level 1), Energy Ball (level 1), Cotton Guard (level 1), Fairy Wind (level 1). It belongs to the Plant and Fairy egg groups.

title: Petilil
text: Petilil is a Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Bulb Pokémon. The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body. Since they prefer moist, nutrient-rich soil, the areas where Petilil live are known to be good for growing plants. The leaves on its head grow right back even if they fall out. These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them. Although the leaves on its head are bitter enough to cause dizziness, they provide relief from weariness—even more so when boiled. By pruning the leaves on its head with regularity, this Pokémon can be grown into a fine plump shape. Some say if you dry the leaves on its head, boil them down, and drink the infusion, your vigor will return, so Petilil is popular with the elderly. Its base stats are 45 HP, 35 Attack, 50 Defense, 70 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 30 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 280. It has the abilities Chlorophyll and Own Tempo, with the hidden ability Leaf Guard. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 6.6 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Stun Spore (level 22), Giga Drain (level 26), Aromatherapy (level 28), Helping Hand (level 31), Energy Ball (level 35), Recover (level 37). It belongs to the Plant egg groups.

title: Lilligant
text: Lilligant is a Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Flowering Pokémon. It evolves from Petilil. Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities. The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect. It withers if a Trainer does not take good care of it. The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult. No matter how much time and money is spent raising it, its flowers are the most beautiful when they bloom in the wild. As soon as it finds a male to be its partner, the beautiful flower on its head darkens, droops, and withers away. The fragrance of its flower differs slightly depending on the soil where it grew up. Sniffing the aroma calms your heart and mind. Its base stats are 70 HP, 60 Attack, 75 Defense, 110 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 90 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 480. It has the abilities Chlorophyll and Own Tempo, with the hidden ability Leaf Guard. It stands 1.1 meters tall and weighs 16.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Aromatherapy (level 1), Magical Leaf (level 1), Energy Ball (level 1), Leaf Storm (level 1), Entrainment (level 1), After You (level 1). It belongs to the Plant egg groups.

title: Basculin red striped
text: Basculin red striped is a Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Hostile Pokémon. Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they’ll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile. Red and blue Basculin usually do not get along, but sometimes members of one school mingle with the other’s school. Red- and blue-striped Basculin are very violent and always fighting. They are also remarkably tasty. Savage, violent Pokémon, red and blue Basculin are always fighting each other over territory. When a school of Basculin appears in a lake, everything else disappears, except for Corphish and Crawdaunt. That’s how violent Basculin are. Anglers love the fight this Pokémon puts up on the hook. And there are always more to catch— many people release them into lakes illicitly. Its base stats are 70 HP, 92 Attack, 65 Defense, 80 Special Attack, 55 Special Defense, 98 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 460. It has the abilities Reckless and Adaptability, with the hidden ability Mold Breaker. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 18.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Crunch (level 24), Aqua Tail (level 28), Soak (level 32), Double Edge (level 36), Scary Face (level 41), Wave Crash (level 44). It belongs to the Water2 egg groups.

title: Sandile
text: Sandile is a Ground and Dark type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Desert Croc Pokémon. They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping. It moves along below the sand’s surface, except for its nose and eyes. A dark membrane shields its eyes from the sun. They live hidden under hot desert sands in order to keep their body temperature from dropping. It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature. It conceals itself in the sand and chomps down on the legs of any prey that unwarily walk over it. Its favorite food is Trapinch. Alola, where it’s warm all year round, is a comfortable environment for this Pokémon. Sometimes you’ll even see it outside of deserts. Its base stats are 50 HP, 72 Attack, 35 Defense, 35 Special Attack, 35 Special Defense, 65 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 292. It has the abilities Intimidate and Moxie, with the hidden ability Anger Point. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 15.2 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Mud Slap (level 19), Embargo (level 22), Swagger (level 25), Crunch (level 28), Dig (level 31), Scary Face (level 34). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Krokorok
text: Krokorok is a Ground and Dark type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Desert Croc Pokémon. It evolves from Sandile. They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms. The special membrane covering its eyes can sense the heat of objects, so it can see its surroundings even in darkness. Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals. They move in groups of a few individuals. A female is often the leader of the group, and the males will gather food. Thanks to the special membrane covering its eyes, it can see its surroundings clearly, even in the middle of the night. Krokorok really hates it when its body gets cold. On nights when the temperature drops, it digs deep into the desert sands. Its base stats are 60 HP, 82 Attack, 45 Defense, 45 Special Attack, 45 Special Defense, 74 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 351. It has the abilities Intimidate and Moxie, with the hidden ability Anger Point. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 33.4 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Mud Slap (level 19), Embargo (level 22), Swagger (level 25), Crunch (level 28), Dig (level 32), Scary Face (level 36). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Krookodile
text: Krookodile is a Ground and Dark type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Intimidation Pokémon. It evolves from Krokorok. They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile. It can expand the focus of its eyes, enabling it to see objects in the far distance as if it were using binoculars. Very violent Pokémon, they try to clamp down on anything that moves in front of their eyes. Its unique faculty of sight can detect small prey more than 30 miles away, even in the midst of a sandstorm. After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half. When it spots prey, even at a distance of over 30 miles, it swims through the desert as if it were water, then jumps out and chomps them. Its base stats are 95 HP, 117 Attack, 80 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 70 Special Defense, 92 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 519. It has the abilities Intimidate and Moxie, with the hidden ability Anger Point. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 96.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Embargo (level 22), Swagger (level 25), Crunch (level 28), Dig (level 32), Scary Face (level 36), Foul Play (level 42). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Darumaka
text: Darumaka is a Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Zen Charm Pokémon. When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep. Darumaka’s droppings are hot, so people used to put them in their clothes to keep themselves warm. When it sleeps, it pulls its limbs into its body and its internal fire goes down to 1,100° F. When it sleeps, it pulls its limbs into its body and its internal fire goes down to 1,100 degrees Fahrenheit. It derives its power from fire burning inside its body. If the fire dwindles, this Pokémon will immediately fall asleep. This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled. Its base stats are 70 HP, 90 Attack, 45 Defense, 15 Special Attack, 45 Special Defense, 50 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 315. It has the abilities Hustle, with the hidden ability Inner Focus. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 37.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Uproar (level 17), Facade (level 19), Fire Punch (level 22), Work Up (level 25), Thrash (level 27), Belly Drum (level 30). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Darmanitan standard
text: Darmanitan standard is a Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Blazing Pokémon. It evolves from Darumaka. Its internal fire burns at 2,500° F, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch. When weakened in battle, it transforms into a stone statue. Then it sharpens its mind and fights on mentally. When one is injured in a fierce battle, it hardens into a stone-like form. Then it meditates and sharpens its mind. Its internal fire burns at 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch. The thick arms of this hot-blooded Pokémon can deliver punches capable of obliterating a dump truck. This Pokémon’s power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit. Its base stats are 105 HP, 140 Attack, 55 Defense, 30 Special Attack, 55 Special Defense, 95 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 480. It has the abilities Sheer Force, with the hidden ability Zen Mode. It stands 1.3 meters tall and weighs 92.9 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Fire Punch (level 22), Work Up (level 25), Thrash (level 27), Belly Drum (level 30), Uproar (level 32), Flare Blitz (level 33). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Maractus
text: Maractus is a Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Cactus Pokémon. It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds. Arid regions are their habitat. They move rhythmically, making a sound similar to maracas. When it moves rhythmically, it makes a sound similar to maracas, making the surprised Pokémon flee. With noises that could be mistaken for the rattles of maracas, it creates an upbeat rhythm, startling bird Pokémon and making them fly off in a hurry. Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They’re jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert. Its base stats are 75 HP, 86 Attack, 67 Defense, 106 Special Attack, 67 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 461. It has the abilities Water Absorb and Chlorophyll, with the hidden ability Storm Drain. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 28.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Giga Drain (level 26), Acupressure (level 29), Ingrain (level 33), Petal Dance (level 38), Sucker Punch (level 42), Sunny Day (level 45). It belongs to the Plant egg groups.

title: Dwebble
text: Dwebble is a Bug and Rock type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Rock Inn Pokémon. This Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth. It makes a hole in a suitable rock. If that rock breaks, the Pokémon remains agitated until it locates a replacement. When it finds a stone of a suitable size, it secretes a liquid from its mouth to open up a hole to crawl into. When it finds a stone appealing, it creates a hole inside it and uses it as its home. This Pokémon is the natural enemy of Roggenrola and Rolycoly. It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon. Its base stats are 50 HP, 65 Attack, 85 Defense, 35 Special Attack, 35 Special Defense, 55 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 325. It has the abilities Sturdy and Shell Armor, with the hidden ability Weak Armor. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 14.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Smack Down (level 17), Rock Polish (level 19), Bug Bite (level 23), Stealth Rock (level 24), Rock Slide (level 29), Slash (level 31). It belongs to the Bug and Mineral egg groups.

title: Crustle
text: Crustle is a Bug and Rock type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Stone Home Pokémon. It evolves from Dwebble. Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle. It possesses legs of enormous strength, enabling it to carry heavy slabs for many days, even when crossing arid land. When its boulder is broken in battles for territory, it feels unsure and begins to weaken. This highly territorial Pokémon prefers dry climates. It won’t come out of its boulder on rainy days. Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They’re mighty enough to crack Rhyperior’s carapace. Its base stats are 70 HP, 105 Attack, 125 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 45 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 485. It has the abilities Sturdy and Shell Armor, with the hidden ability Weak Armor. It stands 1.4 meters tall and weighs 200.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Feint Attack (level 13), Smack Down (level 17), Rock Polish (level 19), Bug Bite (level 23), Stealth Rock (level 24), Rock Slide (level 29). It belongs to the Bug and Mineral egg groups.

title: Scraggy
text: Scraggy is a Dark and Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Shedding Pokémon. Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck. It immediately headbutts anyone that makes eye contact with it. Its skull is massively thick. Proud of its sturdy skull, it suddenly headbutts everything, but its weight makes it unstable, too. They move in small groups, walking around lazily. Anyone who makes eye contact gets smacked with a headbutt. It stretches its saggy skin up to its neck to protect itself. The saggier their skin, the more respect they garner. If it locks eyes with you, watch out! Nothing and no one is safe from the reckless headbutts of this troublesome Pokémon. Its base stats are 50 HP, 75 Attack, 70 Defense, 35 Special Attack, 70 Special Defense, 48 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 348. It has the abilities Shed Skin and Moxie, with the hidden ability Intimidate. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 11.8 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Payback (level 23), Beat Up (level 24), Chip Away (level 27), High Jump Kick (level 31), Scary Face (level 34), Crunch (level 38). It belongs to the Ground and Dragon egg groups.

title: Scrafty
text: Scrafty is a Dark and Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Hoodlum Pokémon. It evolves from Scraggy. Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth. It can smash concrete blocks with its kicking attacks. The one with the biggest crest is the group leader. It pulls up its shed skin to protect itself while it kicks. The bigger the crest, the more respected it is. This Pokémon may be rude, but it takes very good care of its family, its friends, and its turf. It taunts its opponents by spitting. It has a certain territory that it never leaves its whole life long. As halfhearted as this Pokémon’s kicks may seem, they pack enough power to shatter Conkeldurr’s concrete pillars. Its base stats are 65 HP, 90 Attack, 115 Defense, 45 Special Attack, 115 Special Defense, 58 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 488. It has the abilities Shed Skin and Moxie, with the hidden ability Intimidate. It stands 1.1 meters tall and weighs 30.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Payback (level 23), Beat Up (level 24), Chip Away (level 27), High Jump Kick (level 31), Scary Face (level 34), Crunch (level 38). It belongs to the Ground and Dragon egg groups.

title: Sigilyph
text: Sigilyph is a Psychic and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Avianoid Pokémon. They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast. The guardians of an ancient city, they use their psychic power to attack enemies that invade their territory. The guardians of an ancient city, they always fly the same route while keeping watch for invaders. Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians’ emissaries. A discovery was made in the desert where Sigilyph fly. The ruins of what may have been an ancient city were found beneath the sands. Its base stats are 72 HP, 58 Attack, 80 Defense, 103 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 97 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 490. It has the abilities Wonder Skin and Magic Guard, with the hidden ability Tinted Lens. It stands 1.4 meters tall and weighs 14.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Light Screen (level 24), Reflect (level 28), Synchronoise (level 31), Mirror Move (level 34), Gravity (level 38), Air Slash (level 41). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Yamask
text: Yamask is a Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Spirit Pokémon. Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry. These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves in past ages. Each retains memories of its former life. These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves. Each retains memories of its former life. It wanders through ruins by night, carrying a mask that’s said to have been the face it had when it was still human. The spirit of a person from a bygone age became this Pokémon. It rambles through ruins, searching for someone who knows its face. Its base stats are 38 HP, 30 Attack, 85 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 65 Special Defense, 30 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 303. It has the abilities Mummy. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 1.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Will O Wisp (level 21), Ominous Wind (level 25), Curse (level 29), Guard Split (level 33), Power Split (level 33), Shadow Ball (level 37). It belongs to the Mineral and Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Cofagrigus
text: Cofagrigus is a Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Coffin Pokémon. It evolves from Yamask. It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets. They pretend to be elaborate coffins to teach lessons to grave robbers. Their bodies are covered in pure gold. Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies. This Pokémon has a body of sparkling gold. People say it no longer remembers that it was once human. There are many depictions of Cofagrigus decorating ancient tombs. They’re symbols of the wealth that kings of bygone eras had. Its base stats are 58 HP, 50 Attack, 145 Defense, 95 Special Attack, 105 Special Defense, 30 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 483. It has the abilities Mummy. It stands 1.7 meters tall and weighs 76.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Ominous Wind (level 25), Curse (level 29), Guard Split (level 33), Power Split (level 33), Scary Face (level 34), Shadow Ball (level 39). It belongs to the Mineral and Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Tirtouga
text: Tirtouga is a Water and Rock type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Prototurtle Pokémon. Restored from a fossil, this Pokémon can dive to depths beyond half a mile. About 100 million years ago, these Pokémon swam in oceans. It is thought they also went on land to attack prey. This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. It swam skillfully and dove to depths beyond half a mile. Based on studies of its skeletal structure, it can apparently dive to ocean depths of over half a mile. Reputed to be the ancestor of most turtle Pokémon, it lived in warm seas approximately a hundred million years ago. It was restored from an ancient fossil. Tirtouga lived in the sea but came up onto the land to search for prey. Its base stats are 54 HP, 78 Attack, 103 Defense, 53 Special Attack, 45 Special Defense, 22 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 355. It has the abilities Solid Rock and Sturdy, with the hidden ability Swift Swim. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 16.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Wide Guard (level 25), Brine (level 28), Smack Down (level 31), Iron Defense (level 33), Curse (level 35), Shell Smash (level 38). It belongs to the Water1 and Water3 egg groups.

title: Carracosta
text: Carracosta is a Water and Rock type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Prototurtle Pokémon. It evolves from Tirtouga. They can live both in the ocean and on land. A slap from one of them is enough to open a hole in the bottom of a tanker. Incredible jaw strength enables them to chew up steel beams and rocks along with their prey. It could knock out a foe with a slap from one of its developed front appendages and chew it up, shell or bones and all. It constructed its sturdy shell by crunching and swallowing the hard shells or bones of its prey. Active both on land and in the sea, this Pokémon drags its land-based prey into the water to finish it off. Its jaws are terrifyingly powerful. It could eat Omastar and Omanyte whole and not be bothered in the slightest by their shells. Its base stats are 74 HP, 108 Attack, 133 Defense, 83 Special Attack, 65 Special Defense, 32 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 495. It has the abilities Solid Rock and Sturdy, with the hidden ability Swift Swim. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 81.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Wide Guard (level 25), Brine (level 28), Smack Down (level 31), Iron Defense (level 33), Curse (level 35), Shell Smash (level 40). It belongs to the Water1 and Water3 egg groups.

title: Archen
text: Archen is a Rock and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the First Bird Pokémon. Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another. Revived from a fossil, this Pokémon is thought to be the ancestor of all bird Pokémon. It was revived from an ancient fossil. Not able to fly, it lived in treetops and hopped from one branch to another. It is the ancestor of all bird Pokémon. Archen itself could not actually fly but moved by hopping from treetop to treetop. To all appearances flightless, it was able to glide down from tall treetops to snag its prey. Once thought to be the ancestor of all bird Pokémon, some of the latest research suggests that may not be the case. Its base stats are 55 HP, 112 Attack, 45 Defense, 74 Special Attack, 45 Special Defense, 70 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 401. It has the abilities Defeatist. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 9.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Quick Guard (level 25), Acrobatics (level 28), Dragon Breath (level 31), Crunch (level 35), Tailwind (level 36), Endeavor (level 38). It belongs to the Flying and Water3 egg groups.

title: Archeops
text: Archeops is a Rock and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the First Bird Pokémon. It evolves from Archen. They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight. It runs better than it flies. It catches prey by running at speeds comparable to those of an automobile. It runs better than it flies. It takes off into the sky by running at a speed of 25 mph. Although apparently able to fly, they tended to run along the ground, averaging speeds of roughly 25 mph. They hunted in flocks. When one Archeops had the prey cornered, another would swoop on it. This ancient Pokémon’s plumage is delicate, so if anyone other than an experienced professional tries to restore it, they will fail. Its base stats are 75 HP, 140 Attack, 65 Defense, 112 Special Attack, 65 Special Defense, 110 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 567. It has the abilities Defeatist. It stands 1.4 meters tall and weighs 32.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Quick Guard (level 25), Acrobatics (level 28), Dragon Breath (level 31), Crunch (level 35), Tailwind (level 36), Endeavor (level 40). It belongs to the Flying and Water3 egg groups.

title: Trubbish
text: Trubbish is a Poison type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Trash Bag Pokémon. Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places. The combination of garbage bags and industrial waste caused the chemical reaction that created this Pokémon. Wanting more garbage, they follow people who litter. They always belch poison gas. Unsanitary places are what they like best. They can be spotted in Alola, often with Grimer in hot pursuit. It gorges on trash until its stomach is full. Then it belches toxic gas. An unlucky whiff of gas will put a person in the hospital. Poisonous gas leaks out of it when it breathes. Muk that catch a whiff of that stench will come drooling. Its base stats are 50 HP, 50 Attack, 62 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 62 Special Defense, 65 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 329. It has the abilities Stench and Sticky Hold, with the hidden ability Aftermath. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 31.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Swallow (level 23), Take Down (level 25), Sludge Bomb (level 29), Clear Smog (level 34), Toxic (level 36), Pain Split (level 37). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Garbodor
text: Garbodor is a Poison type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Trash Heap Pokémon. It evolves from Trubbish. It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth. They absorb garbage and make it part of their bodies. They shoot a poisonous liquid from their right-hand fingertips. Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies. Beware the poisonous liquid it shoots from its right arm. If even a little of it gets on you, you’ll experience the effects of the unidentified toxin. For a time, their numbers increased explosively in Alola. Since the arrival of Grimer, their population has decreased dramatically. It locks opponents in place with its left hand, immobilizing them by entirely dousing their bodies with poisonous liquid. Its base stats are 80 HP, 95 Attack, 82 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 82 Special Defense, 75 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 474. It has the abilities Stench and Weak Armor, with the hidden ability Aftermath. It stands 1.9 meters tall and weighs 107.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Swallow (level 23), Body Slam (level 25), Sludge Bomb (level 29), Clear Smog (level 34), Toxic (level 39), Pain Split (level 39). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Zorua
text: Zorua is a Dark type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Tricky Fox Pokémon. It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child. To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon. It changes so it looks just like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee. This Pokémon has a cowardly disposition, so when it’s not around friends, it basically always stays transformed as something else. If a normally talkative child suddenly stops talking, it may have been replaced by Zorua. Zorua is a timid Pokémon. This disposition seems to be what led to the development of Zorua’s ability to take on the forms of other creatures. Its base stats are 40 HP, 65 Attack, 40 Defense, 80 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 65 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 330. It has the abilities Illusion. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 12.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Knock Off (level 24), Taunt (level 25), Foul Play (level 29), Torment (level 33), Agility (level 37), Embargo (level 41). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Zoroark
text: Zoroark is a Dark type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Illusion Fox Pokémon. It evolves from Zorua. Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents. Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery. Stories say those who tried to catch Zoroark were trapped in an illusion and punished. It doesn’t just transform itself—it also has the power to make hundreds of people see its illusions. If it thinks humans are going to discover its den, Zoroark shows them visions that make them wander around in the woods. This Pokémon cares deeply about others of its kind, and it will conjure terrifying illusions to keep its den and pack safe. Its base stats are 60 HP, 105 Attack, 60 Defense, 120 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 105 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 510. It has the abilities Illusion. It stands 1.6 meters tall and weighs 81.1 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Fake Tears (level 28), Foul Play (level 29), Night Slash (level 30), Torment (level 34), Agility (level 39), Embargo (level 44). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Minccino
text: Minccino is a Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Chinchilla Pokémon. They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean. These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms. Minccino greet each other by grooming one another thoroughly with their tails. A clean freak that will not allow even the slightest mess, it uses its tail like a mop to thoroughly clean any and all filth. When its tail has gotten dirty from self-cleaning or from cleaning its nest, Minccino spends a whole day washing its tail in clean spring water. The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle. Its base stats are 55 HP, 50 Attack, 40 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 75 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 300. It has the abilities Cute Charm and Technician, with the hidden ability Skill Link. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 5.8 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Sing (level 21), Tail Slap (level 25), Charm (level 27), Wake Up Slap (level 31), Echoed Voice (level 33), Slam (level 37). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Cinccino
text: Cinccino is a Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Scarf Pokémon. It evolves from Minccino. Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks. Their white fur feels amazing to touch. Their fur repels dust and prevents static electricity from building up. Cinccino’s body is coated in a special oil that helps it deflect attacks, such as punches. The white parts of its coat are covered in an oil secreted from its body, so this Pokémon can slide right away from its enemies’ attacks. The oil that seeps from its body is really smooth. For people troubled by bad skin, this oil is an effective treatment. Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff. Its base stats are 75 HP, 95 Attack, 60 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 115 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 470. It has the abilities Cute Charm and Technician, with the hidden ability Skill Link. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 7.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Helping Hand (level 1), Hyper Voice (level 1), Tickle (level 1), Bullet Seed (level 1), Rock Blast (level 1), Last Resort (level 1). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Gothita
text: Gothita is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Fixation Pokémon. Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something. They intently observe both Trainers and Pokémon. Apparently, they are looking at something that only Gothita can see. It stares intently at everything. It can become so obsessed with watching that it doesn’t notice attacks. Though they’re still only babies, there’s psychic power stored in their ribbonlike feelers, and sometimes they use that power to fight. Even when nobody seems to be around, Gothita can still be heard making a muted cry. Many believe it’s speaking to something only it can see. Its base stats are 45 HP, 30 Attack, 50 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 65 Special Defense, 45 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 290. It has the abilities Frisk and Competitive, with the hidden ability Shadow Tag. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 5.8 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Feint Attack (level 24), Psyshock (level 25), Flatter (level 28), Future Sight (level 31), Psych Up (level 33), Heal Block (level 33). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Gothorita
text: Gothorita is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Manipulate Pokémon. It evolves from Gothita. They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner. Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones. According to many old tales, it creates friends for itself by controlling sleeping children on starry nights. It’s said that when stars shine in the night sky, this Pokémon will spirit away sleeping children. Some call it the Witch of Punishment. On nights when the stars shine, this Pokémon’s psychic power is at its strongest. It’s unknown just what link Gothorita has to the greater universe. Its base stats are 60 HP, 45 Attack, 70 Defense, 75 Special Attack, 85 Special Defense, 55 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 390. It has the abilities Frisk and Competitive, with the hidden ability Shadow Tag. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 18.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Feint Attack (level 24), Psyshock (level 25), Flatter (level 28), Future Sight (level 31), Heal Block (level 34), Psych Up (level 35). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Gothitelle
text: Gothitelle is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Astral Body Pokémon. It evolves from Gothorita. Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power. They can predict the future from the placement and movement of the stars. They can see Trainers’ life spans. It can see the future from the movement of the stars. When it learns its Trainer’s life span, it cries in sadness. It has tremendous psychic power, but it dislikes conflict. It’s also able to predict the future based on the movement of the stars. A criminal who was shown his fate by a Gothitelle went missing that same day and was never seen again. Its base stats are 70 HP, 55 Attack, 95 Defense, 95 Special Attack, 110 Special Defense, 65 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 490. It has the abilities Frisk and Competitive, with the hidden ability Shadow Tag. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 44.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Feint Attack (level 24), Psyshock (level 25), Flatter (level 28), Future Sight (level 31), Heal Block (level 34), Psych Up (level 35). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Solosis
text: Solosis is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Cell Pokémon. They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others. Because their bodies are enveloped in a special liquid, they can survive in any environment. Because their bodies are enveloped in a special liquid, they are fine in any environment, no matter how severe. It communicates with others telepathically. Its body is encapsulated in liquid, but if it takes a heavy blow, the liquid will leak out. Many say that the special liquid covering this Pokémon’s body would allow it to survive in the vacuum of space. Its base stats are 45 HP, 30 Attack, 40 Defense, 105 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 20 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 290. It has the abilities Overcoat and Magic Guard, with the hidden ability Regenerator. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 1.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Recover (level 24), Psyshock (level 25), Endeavor (level 28), Ally Switch (level 28), Future Sight (level 31), Pain Split (level 33). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Duosion
text: Duosion is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Mitosis Pokémon. It evolves from Solosis. Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once. When their brains, now divided in two, are thinking the same thoughts, these Pokémon exhibit their maximum power. When their two divided brains think the same thoughts, their psychic power is maximized. Its psychic power can supposedly cover a range of more than half a mile—but only if its two brains can agree with each other. Its brain has split into two, and the two halves rarely think alike. Its actions are utterly unpredictable. Its base stats are 65 HP, 40 Attack, 50 Defense, 125 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 30 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 370. It has the abilities Overcoat and Magic Guard, with the hidden ability Regenerator. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 8.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Recover (level 24), Psyshock (level 25), Endeavor (level 28), Ally Switch (level 28), Future Sight (level 31), Pain Split (level 34). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Reuniclus
text: Reuniclus is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Multiplying Pokémon. It evolves from Duosion. When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power. These remarkably intelligent Pokémon fight by controlling arms that can grip with rock-crushing power. They use psychic power to control their arms, which are made of a special liquid. They can crush boulders psychically. While it could use its psychic abilities in battle, this Pokémon prefers to swing its powerful arms around to beat opponents into submission. It’s said that drinking the liquid surrounding Reuniclus grants wisdom. Problem is, the liquid is highly toxic to anything besides Reuniclus itself. Its base stats are 110 HP, 65 Attack, 75 Defense, 125 Special Attack, 85 Special Defense, 30 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 490. It has the abilities Overcoat and Magic Guard, with the hidden ability Regenerator. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 20.1 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Psyshock (level 25), Endeavor (level 28), Ally Switch (level 28), Future Sight (level 31), Pain Split (level 34), Psychic (level 39). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Ducklett
text: Ducklett is a Water and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Water Bird Pokémon. These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food--peat moss. When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray. They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater. Its base stats are 62 HP, 44 Attack, 50 Defense, 44 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 55 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 305. It has the abilities Keen Eye and Big Pecks, with the hidden ability Hydration. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 5.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Aerial Ace (level 15), Bubble Beam (level 19), Feather Dance (level 21), Aqua Ring (level 24), Air Slash (level 27), Roost (level 30). It belongs to the Water1 and Flying egg groups.

title: Swanna
text: Swanna is a Water and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the White Bird Pokémon. It evolves from Ducklett. Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock. It administers sharp, powerful pecks with its bill. It whips its long neck to deliver forceful repeated strikes. Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles. Its base stats are 75 HP, 87 Attack, 63 Defense, 87 Special Attack, 63 Special Defense, 98 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 473. It has the abilities Keen Eye and Big Pecks, with the hidden ability Hydration. It stands 1.3 meters tall and weighs 24.2 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Aerial Ace (level 15), Bubble Beam (level 19), Feather Dance (level 21), Aqua Ring (level 24), Air Slash (level 27), Roost (level 30). It belongs to the Water1 and Flying egg groups.

title: Vanillite
text: Vanillite is a Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Fresh Snow Pokémon. The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them. This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow. Theoretically, this Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. Their breath is -58° F. The temperature of their breath is -58 degrees Fahrenheit. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them. Born of an icicle, this Pokémon uses its frosty breath to make ice crystals, causing snow to fall. It feels pleasantly cool when embraced. This Pokémon is treasured by households in warm regions. Its base stats are 36 HP, 50 Attack, 50 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 44 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 305. It has the abilities Ice Body and Snow Cloak, with the hidden ability Weak Armor. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 5.7 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Mist (level 16), Avalanche (level 19), Taunt (level 22), Mirror Shot (level 26), Acid Armor (level 31), Ice Beam (level 35). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Vanillish
text: Vanillish is a Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Icy Snow Pokémon. It evolves from Vanillite. Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas. It conceals itself from enemy eyes by creating many small ice particles and hiding among them. They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes. Hot days cause its body to melt. It can be restored by refreezing it, but the process leaves its body slightly warped. This Pokémon has existed since the Ice Age. It controls particles of ice, freezes its opponents, and then shatters them with a headbutt. It sprays its enemies with grains of ice to freeze them. Most Vanillish in Alola are smaller than average. Its base stats are 51 HP, 65 Attack, 65 Defense, 80 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 59 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 395. It has the abilities Ice Body and Snow Cloak, with the hidden ability Weak Armor. It stands 1.1 meters tall and weighs 41.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Mist (level 16), Avalanche (level 19), Taunt (level 22), Mirror Shot (level 26), Acid Armor (level 31), Ice Beam (level 36). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Vanilluxe
text: Vanilluxe is a Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Snowstorm Pokémon. It evolves from Vanillish. Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards. If both heads get angry simultaneously, this Pokémon expels a blizzard, burying everything in snow. Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards. Each of its two heads has a brain, and when they are in agreement, it attacks its enemies by exhaling a violent blizzard. Even if it loses one of its heads, it can live relatively problem-free. It makes snow clouds inside its body. It blasts blizzards from its two mouths. It can create snow anywhere, so it gets a lot of love from skiers and snowboarders. Its base stats are 71 HP, 95 Attack, 85 Defense, 110 Special Attack, 95 Special Defense, 79 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 535. It has the abilities Ice Body and Snow Warning, with the hidden ability Weak Armor. It stands 1.3 meters tall and weighs 57.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Mist (level 16), Avalanche (level 19), Taunt (level 22), Mirror Shot (level 26), Acid Armor (level 31), Ice Beam (level 36). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Deerling
text: Deerling is a Normal and Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Season Pokémon. The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass. The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokémon’s fur. People use it to mark the seasons. Their coloring changes according to the seasons and can be slightly affected by the temperature and humidity as well. Its base stats are 60 HP, 60 Attack, 50 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 75 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 335. It has the abilities Chlorophyll and Sap Sipper, with the hidden ability Serene Grace. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 19.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bullet Seed (level 16), Take Down (level 20), Jump Kick (level 24), Zen Headbutt (level 24), Aromatherapy (level 28), Energy Ball (level 32). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Sawsbuck
text: Sawsbuck is a Normal and Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Season Pokémon. It evolves from Deerling. They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck’s horns. The plants growing on its horns change according to the season. The leaders of the herd possess magnificent horns. They migrate according to the seasons, so some people call Sawsbuck the harbingers of spring. Its base stats are 80 HP, 100 Attack, 70 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 70 Special Defense, 95 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 475. It has the abilities Chlorophyll and Sap Sipper, with the hidden ability Serene Grace. It stands 1.9 meters tall and weighs 92.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Take Down (level 20), Jump Kick (level 24), Zen Headbutt (level 24), Aromatherapy (level 28), Energy Ball (level 32), Charm (level 36). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Emolga
text: Emolga is a Electric and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Sky Squirrel Pokémon. The energy made in its cheeks’ electric pouches is stored inside its membrane and released while it is gliding. They live on treetops and glide using the inside of a cape-like membrane while discharging electricity. It glides on its outstretched membrane while shocking foes with the electricity stored in the pouches on its cheeks. It glides using its cape-like membrane. Electrical energy scatters from it, shocking its friends and foes alike. It grills berries and bug Pokémon with electric shocks and makes a meal of them. It usually nests in the holes gouged in trees by Pikipek. They store up electricity to fly through the air. When thunder cracks at night, sometimes there are almost enough of them to blot out the sky. Its base stats are 55 HP, 75 Attack, 60 Defense, 75 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 103 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 428. It has the abilities Static, with the hidden ability Motor Drive. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 5.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Pursuit (level 16), Double Team (level 19), Shock Wave (level 22), Electro Ball (level 26), Acrobatics (level 30), Light Screen (level 34). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Karrablast
text: Karrablast is a Bug type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Clamping Pokémon. These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet. When they feel threatened, they spit an acidic liquid to drive attackers away. This Pokémon targets Shelmet. For some reason they evolve when they receive electrical energy while they are attacking Shelmet. Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve. It spits a liquid from its mouth to melt through Shelmet’s shell. Karrablast doesn’t eat the shell— it eats only the contents. Its base stats are 50 HP, 75 Attack, 45 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 45 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 315. It has the abilities Swarm and Shed Skin, with the hidden ability No Guard. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 5.9 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Headbutt (level 20), False Swipe (level 25), Bug Buzz (level 28), Slash (level 32), Take Down (level 37), Scary Face (level 40). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Escavalier
text: Escavalier is a Bug and Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Cavalry Pokémon. It evolves from Karrablast. They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely. These Pokémon evolve by wearing the shell covering of a Shelmet. The steel armor protects their whole body. Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances. They use shells they’ve stolen from Shelmet to arm and protect themselves. They’re very popular Pokémon in the Galar region. It charges its enemies, lances at the ready. An image of one of its duels is captured in a famous painting of Escavalier clashing with Sirfetch’d. Its base stats are 70 HP, 135 Attack, 105 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 105 Special Defense, 20 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 495. It has the abilities Swarm and Shell Armor, with the hidden ability Overcoat. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 33.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include False Swipe (level 25), Bug Buzz (level 28), Slash (level 32), Iron Head (level 37), Iron Defense (level 40), X Scissor (level 44). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Foongus
text: Foongus is a Grass and Poison type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Mushroom Pokémon. It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown. For some reason, this Pokémon resembles a Poké Ball. They release poison spores to repel those who try to catch them. It lures Pokémon with its pattern that looks just like a Poké Ball then releases poison spores. It lures Pokémon with its pattern that looks just like a Poké Ball, then releases poison spores.
