No one knows what the Poké Ball–like pattern on Foongus means or why Foongus has it. The spores released from this Pokémon’s hands are highly poisonous, but when thoroughly dried, the spores can be used as stomach medicine. Its base stats are 69 HP, 55 Attack, 45 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 55 Special Defense, 15 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 294. It has the abilities Effect Spore, with the hidden ability Regenerator. It stands 0.2 meters tall and weighs 1.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Ingrain (level 18), Feint Attack (level 20), Sweet Scent (level 24), Giga Drain (level 28), Toxic (level 32), Synthesis (level 35). It belongs to the Plant egg groups.

title: Amoonguss
text: Amoonguss is a Grass and Poison type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Mushroom Pokémon. It evolves from Foongus. It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion. They show off their Poké Ball caps to lure prey, but very few Pokémon are fooled by this. It moves the caps on both arms and does a dance to lure prey. It prefers damp places. This Pokémon puffs poisonous spores at its foes. If the spores aren’t washed off quickly, they’ll grow into mushrooms wherever they land. Amoonguss generally doesn’t move much. It tends to stand still near Poké Balls that have been dropped on the ground. Its base stats are 114 HP, 85 Attack, 70 Defense, 85 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 30 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 464. It has the abilities Effect Spore, with the hidden ability Regenerator. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 10.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Ingrain (level 18), Feint Attack (level 20), Sweet Scent (level 24), Giga Drain (level 28), Toxic (level 32), Synthesis (level 35). It belongs to the Plant egg groups.

title: Frillish male
text: Frillish male is a Water and Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Floating Pokémon. With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor. They paralyze prey with poison, then drag them down to their lairs, five miles below the surface. If its veil-like arms stun and wrap a foe, that foe will be dragged miles below the surface, never to return. It wraps its veillike arms and legs around prey swimming by and drags them down to the depths of the ocean. Using the invisible poison spikes on its veillike arms and legs, it paralyzes its enemies and causes them to drown. It envelops its prey in its veillike arms and draws it down to the deeps, five miles below the ocean’s surface. Its base stats are 55 HP, 40 Attack, 50 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 85 Special Defense, 40 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 335. It has the abilities Water Absorb and Cursed Body, with the hidden ability Damp. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 33.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Ominous Wind (level 27), Shadow Ball (level 32), Brine (level 32), Whirlpool (level 36), Rain Dance (level 37), Hex (level 43). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Jellicent male
text: Jellicent male is a Water and Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Floating Pokémon. It evolves from Frillish. The fate of the ships and crew that wander into Jellicent’s habitat: all sunken, all lost, all vanished. They propel themselves by expelling absorbed seawater from their bodies. Their favorite food is life energy. Its body is mostly seawater. It’s said there’s a castle of ships Jellicent have sunk on the seafloor. Jellicent is always hanging around fancy cruise ships and tankers, hoping to drag away its prey. Fishermen are terrified of Jellicent. It’s rumored to drag them into the sea and steal their lives away. Most of this Pokémon’s body composition is identical to sea water. It makes sunken ships its lair. Its base stats are 100 HP, 60 Attack, 70 Defense, 85 Special Attack, 105 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 480. It has the abilities Water Absorb and Cursed Body, with the hidden ability Damp. It stands 2.2 meters tall and weighs 135.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Ominous Wind (level 27), Shadow Ball (level 32), Brine (level 32), Whirlpool (level 36), Rain Dance (level 37), Hex (level 45). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Alomomola
text: Alomomola is a Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Caring Pokémon. The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds. Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore. It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds. It uses its special mucus to close the wounds of injured Pokémon. The reason for this behavior remains unknown. They float upon the open sea. Many water Pokémon gather in the area around Alomomola. Fishermen take them along on long voyages, because if you have an Alomomola with you, there’ll be no need for a doctor or medicine. Its base stats are 165 HP, 75 Attack, 80 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 45 Special Defense, 65 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 470. It has the abilities Healer and Hydration, with the hidden ability Regenerator. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 31.6 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Wake Up Slap (level 29), Soak (level 33), Wish (level 37), Brine (level 41), Safeguard (level 45), Whirlpool (level 49). It belongs to the Water1 and Water2 egg groups.

title: Joltik
text: Joltik is a Bug and Electric type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Attaching Pokémon. Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses. They attach themselves to large-bodied Pokémon and absorb static electricity, which they store in an electric pouch. Since it can’t generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity. Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It’s soaking up static electricity because it can’t produce a charge on its own. Joltik latch on to other Pokémon and suck out static electricity. They’re often found sticking to Yamper’s hindquarters. Its base stats are 50 HP, 47 Attack, 50 Defense, 57 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 65 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 319. It has the abilities Compound Eyes and Unnerve, with the hidden ability Swarm. It stands 0.1 meters tall and weighs 0.6 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Electroweb (level 15), Bug Bite (level 18), Gastro Acid (level 23), Slash (level 26), Electro Ball (level 29), Signal Beam (level 34). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Galvantula
text: Galvantula is a Bug and Electric type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the EleSpider Pokémon. It evolves from Joltik. When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads. They employ an electrically charged web to trap their prey. While it is immobilized by shock, they leisurely consume it. It creates barriers from electrified silk that stun foes. This works as a weapon as well as a defense. It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis. It lays traps of electrified threads near the nests of bird Pokémon, aiming to snare chicks that are not yet good at flying. Its base stats are 70 HP, 77 Attack, 60 Defense, 97 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 108 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 472. It has the abilities Compound Eyes and Unnerve, with the hidden ability Swarm. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 14.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Electroweb (level 15), Bug Bite (level 18), Gastro Acid (level 23), Slash (level 26), Electro Ball (level 29), Signal Beam (level 34). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Ferroseed
text: Ferroseed is a Grass and Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Thorn Seed Pokémon. When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away. They stick their spikes into cave walls and absorb the minerals they find in the rock. It absorbs the iron it finds in the rock while clinging to the ceiling. It shoots spikes when in danger. It defends itself by launching spikes, but its aim isn’t very good at first. Only after a lot of practice will it improve. Mossy caves are their preferred dwellings. Enzymes contained in mosses help Ferroseed’s spikes grow big and strong. Its base stats are 44 HP, 50 Attack, 91 Defense, 24 Special Attack, 86 Special Defense, 10 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 305. It has the abilities Iron Barbs. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 18.8 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Pin Missile (level 18), Gyro Ball (level 21), Iron Defense (level 26), Mirror Shot (level 30), Ingrain (level 35), Self Destruct (level 38). It belongs to the Plant and Mineral egg groups.

title: Ferrothorn
text: Ferrothorn is a Grass and Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Thorn Pod Pokémon. It evolves from Ferroseed. It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble. They attach themselves to cave ceilings, firing steel spikes at targets passing beneath them. By swinging around its three spiky feelers and shooting spikes, it can obliterate an opponent. This Pokémon scrapes its spikes across rocks, and then uses the tips of its feelers to absorb the nutrients it finds within the stone. Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes on its feelers into the stone. Its base stats are 74 HP, 94 Attack, 131 Defense, 54 Special Attack, 116 Special Defense, 20 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 489. It has the abilities Iron Barbs, with the hidden ability Anticipation. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 110.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Gyro Ball (level 21), Iron Defense (level 26), Mirror Shot (level 30), Ingrain (level 35), Self Destruct (level 38), Power Whip (level 40). It belongs to the Plant and Mineral egg groups.

title: Klink
text: Klink is a Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Gear Pokémon. The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing. Interlocking two bodies and spinning around generates the energy they need to live. Two bodies comprise a fixed pair. They spin around each other to generate energy. The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other. It’s suspected that Klink were the inspiration behind ancient people’s invention of the first gears. Its base stats are 40 HP, 55 Attack, 70 Defense, 45 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 30 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 300. It has the abilities Plus and Minus, with the hidden ability Clear Body. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 21.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Charge Beam (level 26), Autotomize (level 31), Mirror Shot (level 36), Screech (level 39), Discharge (level 42), Metal Sound (level 45). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Klang
text: Klang is a Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Gear Pokémon. It evolves from Klink. By changing the direction in which it rotates, it communicates its feelings to others. When angry, it rotates faster. Spinning minigears are rotated at high speed and repeatedly fired away. It is dangerous if the gears don’t return. A minigear and big gear comprise its body. If the minigear it launches at a foe doesn’t return, it will die. When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon’s rotation speed increases sharply. Many companies in the Galar region choose Klang as their logo. This Pokémon is considered the symbol of industrial technology. Its base stats are 60 HP, 80 Attack, 95 Defense, 70 Special Attack, 85 Special Defense, 50 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 440. It has the abilities Plus and Minus, with the hidden ability Clear Body. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 51.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Charge Beam (level 26), Autotomize (level 31), Mirror Shot (level 36), Screech (level 40), Discharge (level 44), Metal Sound (level 48). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Klinklang
text: Klinklang is a Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Gear Pokémon. It evolves from Klang. The gear with the red core is rotated at high speed for a rapid energy charge. Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area. The minigear spins at high speed. Then the energy from the red core charges the minigear to make it ready to fire. From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy. The three gears that compose this Pokémon spin at high speed. Its new spiked gear isn’t a living creature. Its base stats are 60 HP, 100 Attack, 115 Defense, 70 Special Attack, 85 Special Defense, 90 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 520. It has the abilities Plus and Minus, with the hidden ability Clear Body. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 81.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Autotomize (level 31), Mirror Shot (level 36), Screech (level 40), Discharge (level 44), Metal Sound (level 48), Shift Gear (level 54). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Tynamo
text: Tynamo is a Electric type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the EleFish Pokémon. While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning. These Pokémon move in schools. They have an electricity-generating organ, so they discharge electricity if in danger. One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock. Its base stats are 35 HP, 55 Attack, 40 Defense, 45 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 275. It has the abilities Levitate. It stands 0.2 meters tall and weighs 0.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Tackle (level 1), Thunder Wave (level 1), Spark (level 1), Charge Beam (level 1). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Eelektrik
text: Eelektrik is a Electric type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the EleFish Pokémon. It evolves from Tynamo. They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns. These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity. It wraps itself around its prey and paralyzes it with electricity from the round spots on its sides. Then it chomps. Its base stats are 65 HP, 85 Attack, 70 Defense, 75 Special Attack, 70 Special Defense, 40 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 405. It has the abilities Levitate. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 22.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Acid (level 19), Discharge (level 29), Crunch (level 39), Thunderbolt (level 44), Acid Spray (level 49), Coil (level 54). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Eelektross
text: Eelektross is a Electric type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the EleFish Pokémon. It evolves from Eelektrik. They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean. With their sucker mouths, they suck in prey. Then they use their fangs to shock the prey with electricity. It latches on to prey with its sucker mouth, sinking in its fangs and shocking the prey with powerful electricity. Its base stats are 85 HP, 115 Attack, 80 Defense, 105 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 50 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 515. It has the abilities Levitate. It stands 2.1 meters tall and weighs 80.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Thrash (level 1), Acid (level 1), Zap Cannon (level 1), Crunch (level 1), Crush Claw (level 1), Gastro Acid (level 1). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Elgyem
text: Elgyem is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Cerebral Pokémon. It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent’s brain, causing unendurable headaches. This Pokémon had never been seen until it appeared from far in the desert 50 years ago. Rumors of its origin are linked to a UFO crash site in the desert 50 years ago. It controls tremendous psychic power. Most reports of alien sightings are actually just people mistaking Elgyem for an alien. This Pokémon is shrouded in mystery. It’s said to have appeared from a UFO that fell from the sky about 50 years ago. If this Pokémon stands near a TV, strange scenery will appear on the screen. That scenery is said to be from its home. Its base stats are 55 HP, 55 Attack, 55 Defense, 85 Special Attack, 55 Special Defense, 30 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 335. It has the abilities Telepathy and Synchronize, with the hidden ability Analytic. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 9.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Simple Beam (level 29), Zen Headbutt (level 32), Psych Up (level 36), Psychic (level 39), Calm Mind (level 43), Recover (level 46). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Beheeyem
text: Beheeyem is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Cerebral Pokémon. It evolves from Elgyem. It can manipulate an opponent’s memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers. It uses psychic power to control an opponent’s brain and tamper with its memories. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three fingers, but those patterns haven’t been decoded. With its psychic powers, it rewrites its opponents’ memories. You, too, may have already had your memories rewritten. It has strong psychic powers. Using its fingers that flash three different colors, it controls its opponents and rewrites their memories. Whenever a Beheeyem visits a farm, a Dubwool mysteriously disappears. Its base stats are 75 HP, 75 Attack, 75 Defense, 125 Special Attack, 95 Special Defense, 40 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 485. It has the abilities Telepathy and Synchronize, with the hidden ability Analytic. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 34.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Simple Beam (level 29), Zen Headbutt (level 32), Psych Up (level 36), Psychic (level 39), Calm Mind (level 45), Recover (level 50). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Litwick
text: Litwick is a Ghost and Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Candle Pokémon. Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns. While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life force of any who follow it. Its flame is usually out, but it starts shining when it absorbs life force from people or Pokémon. The flame on its head keeps its body slightly warm. This Pokémon takes lost children by the hand to guide them to the spirit world. The younger the life this Pokémon absorbs, the brighter and eerier the flame on its head burns. Its base stats are 50 HP, 30 Attack, 55 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 55 Special Defense, 20 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 275. It has the abilities Flash Fire and Flame Body, with the hidden ability Infiltrator. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 3.1 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Will O Wisp (level 16), Flame Burst (level 20), Imprison (level 24), Hex (level 28), Memento (level 33), Inferno (level 38). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Lampent
text: Lampent is a Ghost and Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Lamp Pokémon. It evolves from Litwick. This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen. It arrives near the moment of death and steals spirit from the body. The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on. This Pokémon appears just before someone passes away, so it’s feared as an emissary of death. It lurks in cities, pretending to be a lamp. Once it finds someone whose death is near, it will trail quietly after them. Its base stats are 60 HP, 40 Attack, 60 Defense, 95 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 55 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 370. It has the abilities Flash Fire and Flame Body, with the hidden ability Infiltrator. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 13.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Will O Wisp (level 16), Flame Burst (level 20), Imprison (level 24), Hex (level 28), Memento (level 33), Inferno (level 38). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Chandelure
text: Chandelure is a Ghost and Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Luring Pokémon. It evolves from Lampent. It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance. Being consumed in Chandelure’s flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind. The spirits burned up in its ominous flame lose their way and wander this world forever. This Pokémon haunts dilapidated mansions. It sways its arms to hypnotize opponents with the ominous dancing of its flames. In homes illuminated by Chandelure instead of lights, funerals were a constant occurrence— or so it’s said. Its base stats are 60 HP, 55 Attack, 90 Defense, 145 Special Attack, 90 Special Defense, 80 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 520. It has the abilities Flash Fire and Flame Body, with the hidden ability Infiltrator. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 34.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Pain Split (level 1), Shadow Ball (level 1), Will O Wisp (level 1), Memento (level 1), Imprison (level 1), Astonish (level 1). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Axew
text: Axew is a Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Tusk Pokémon. They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp. They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly. Its large tusks have a tendency to break, but each time they grow back, they grow in harder and sturdier. These Pokémon nest in the ground and use their tusks to crush hard berries. Crushing berries is also how they test each other’s strength. They play with each other by knocking their large tusks together. Their tusks break sometimes, but they grow back so quickly that it isn’t a concern. Its base stats are 46 HP, 87 Attack, 60 Defense, 30 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 57 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 320. It has the abilities Rivalry and Mold Breaker, with the hidden ability Unnerve. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 18.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Breaking Swipe (level 30), Dragon Dance (level 32), Focus Energy (level 33), Laser Focus (level 33), Taunt (level 36), Dragon Pulse (level 41). It belongs to the Monster and Dragon egg groups.

title: Fraxure
text: Fraxure is a Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Axe Jaw Pokémon. It evolves from Axew. Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they’ve been fighting. Their tusks can shatter rocks. Territory battles between Fraxure can be intensely violent. A broken tusk will not grow back, so it diligently sharpens its tusks on river rocks after the end of a battle. After battle, this Pokémon carefully sharpens its tusks on river rocks. It needs to take care of its tusks—if one breaks, it will never grow back. Its skin is as hard as a suit of armor. Fraxure’s favorite strategy is to tackle its opponents, stabbing them with its tusks at the same time. Its base stats are 66 HP, 117 Attack, 70 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 67 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 410. It has the abilities Rivalry and Mold Breaker, with the hidden ability Unnerve. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 36.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Breaking Swipe (level 30), Dragon Dance (level 32), Focus Energy (level 33), Laser Focus (level 33), Taunt (level 36), Dragon Pulse (level 42). It belongs to the Monster and Dragon egg groups.

title: Haxorus
text: Haxorus is a Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Axe Jaw Pokémon. It evolves from Fraxure. They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel. Their sturdy tusks will stay sharp even if used to cut steel beams. These Pokémon are covered in hard armor. Its tusks are incredibly destructive. They can easily slice through a thick, sturdy steel column every time. Its resilient tusks are its pride and joy. It licks up dirt to take in the minerals it needs to keep its tusks in top condition. While usually kindhearted, it can be terrifying if angered. Tusks that can slice through steel beams are how Haxorus deals with its adversaries. Its base stats are 76 HP, 147 Attack, 90 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 70 Special Defense, 97 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 540. It has the abilities Rivalry and Mold Breaker, with the hidden ability Unnerve. It stands 1.8 meters tall and weighs 105.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Breaking Swipe (level 30), Dragon Dance (level 32), Focus Energy (level 33), Laser Focus (level 33), Taunt (level 36), Dragon Pulse (level 42). It belongs to the Monster and Dragon egg groups.

title: Cubchoo
text: Cubchoo is a Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Chill Pokémon. When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases. Its nose is always running. It sniffs the snot back up because the mucus provides the raw material for its moves. Their snot is a barometer of health. When healthy, their snot is sticky and the power of their ice moves increases. When this Pokémon is in good health, its snot becomes thicker and stickier. It will smear its snot on anyone it doesn’t like. It sniffles before performing a move, using its frosty snot to provide an icy element to any move that needs it. Its base stats are 55 HP, 70 Attack, 40 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 40 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 305. It has the abilities Snow Cloak and Slush Rush, with the hidden ability Rattled. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 8.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Endure (level 25), Charm (level 29), Snowscape (level 30), Slash (level 33), Flail (level 36), Rest (level 41). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Beartic
text: Beartic is a Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Freezing Pokémon. It evolves from Cubchoo. It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey. It freezes its breath to create fangs and claws of ice to fight with. Cold northern areas are its habitat. They love the cold seas of the north. They create pathways across the ocean waters by freezing their own breath. It swims through frigid seas, searching for prey. From its frozen breath, it forms icy fangs that are harder than steel. It swims energetically through frigid seas. When it gets tired, it freezes the seawater with its breath so it can rest on the ice. Its base stats are 95 HP, 130 Attack, 80 Defense, 70 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 50 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 505. It has the abilities Snow Cloak and Slush Rush, with the hidden ability Swift Swim. It stands 2.6 meters tall and weighs 260.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Swagger (level 29), Snowscape (level 30), Slash (level 33), Flail (level 36), Icicle Crash (level 37), Rest (level 41). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Cryogonal
text: Cryogonal is a Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Crystallizing Pokémon. When its body temperature goes up, it turns into steam and vanishes. When its temperature lowers, it returns to ice. They are born in snow clouds. They use chains made of ice crystals to capture prey. They are composed of ice crystals. They capture prey with chains of ice, freezing the prey at -148° F. They are composed of ice crystals. They capture prey with chains of ice, freezing the prey at -148 degrees Fahrenheit. With its icy chains, Cryogonal freezes those it encounters. It then takes its victims away to somewhere unknown. When the weather gets hot, these Pokémon turn into water vapor. Cryogonal are almost never seen during summer. Its base stats are 80 HP, 50 Attack, 50 Defense, 95 Special Attack, 135 Special Defense, 105 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 515. It has the abilities Levitate. It stands 1.1 meters tall and weighs 148.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Ice Beam (level 33), Light Screen (level 37), Reflect (level 37), Slash (level 41), Confuse Ray (level 45), Recover (level 49). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Shelmet
text: Shelmet is a Bug type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Snail Pokémon. When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid. It evolves when bathed in an electric-like energy along with Karrablast. The reason is still unknown. When it and Karrablast are together, and both receive electrical stimulation, they both evolve. When attacked, it tightly shuts the lid of its shell. This reaction fails to protect it from Karrablast, however, because they can still get into the shell. It has a strange physiology that responds to electricity. When together with Karrablast, Shelmet evolves for some reason. Its base stats are 50 HP, 40 Attack, 85 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 65 Special Defense, 25 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 305. It has the abilities Hydration and Shell Armor, with the hidden ability Overcoat. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 7.7 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Mega Drain (level 20), Yawn (level 25), Protect (level 28), Acid Armor (level 32), Giga Drain (level 37), Body Slam (level 40). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Accelgor
text: Accelgor is a Bug type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Shell Out Pokémon. It evolves from Shelmet. When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane. Having removed its heavy shell, it becomes very light and can fight with ninja-like movements. When its body dries out, it weakens. So it wraps a membrane around itself for protection while it spits poison. It moves with blinding speed and lobs poison at foes. Featuring Accelgor as a main character is a surefire way to make a movie or comic popular. Discarding its shell made it nimble. To keep itself from dehydrating, it wraps its body in bands of membrane. Its base stats are 80 HP, 70 Attack, 40 Defense, 100 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 145 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 495. It has the abilities Hydration and Sticky Hold, with the hidden ability Unburden. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 25.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Swift (level 25), Me First (level 28), Agility (level 32), Giga Drain (level 37), U Turn (level 40), Bug Buzz (level 44). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Stunfisk
text: Stunfisk is a Ground and Electric type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Trap Pokémon. Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity. It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity. When its opponent can’t be paralyzed, it contorts itself with unexpected speed and flops away. Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity. For some reason, this Pokémon smiles slightly when it emits a strong electric current from the yellow markings on its body. Its base stats are 109 HP, 66 Attack, 84 Defense, 81 Special Attack, 99 Special Defense, 32 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 471. It has the abilities Static and Limber, with the hidden ability Sand Veil. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 11.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Sucker Punch (level 25), Discharge (level 25), Endure (level 30), Electric Terrain (level 30), Bounce (level 35), Muddy Water (level 40). It belongs to the Water1 and Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Mienfoo
text: Mienfoo is a Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Martial Arts Pokémon. In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies. They have mastered elegant combos. As they concentrate, their battle moves become swifter and more precise. It takes pride in the speed at which it can use moves. What it loses in power, it makes up for in quantity. They can often be seen in packs in the morning, slowly moving their bodies through a series of poses. They seclude themselves in the mountains and devote themselves to training. The form of their kicks and chops differs from pack to pack. In one minute, a well-trained Mienfoo can chop with its arms more than 100 times. Its base stats are 45 HP, 85 Attack, 50 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 65 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 350. It has the abilities Inner Focus and Regenerator, with the hidden ability Reckless. It stands 0.9 meters tall and weighs 20.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Force Palm (level 29), Drain Punch (level 33), Jump Kick (level 37), Hone Claws (level 40), U Turn (level 41), Quick Guard (level 45). It belongs to the Ground and Humanshape egg groups.

title: Mienshao
text: Mienshao is a Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Martial Arts Pokémon. It evolves from Mienfoo. It wields the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen. They use the long fur on their arms as a whip to strike their opponents. Using the long fur on its arms like whips, it launches into combo attacks that, once started, no one can stop. It overwhelms its opponents with continuous attacks and then slowly stores up power before delivering the finishing blow. When Mienshao lets out a bizarre wail, you’re in danger. A flurry of kicks and chops too fast to see is about to be unleashed! When Mienshao comes across a truly challenging opponent, it will lighten itself by biting off the fur on its arms. Its base stats are 65 HP, 125 Attack, 60 Defense, 95 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 105 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 510. It has the abilities Inner Focus and Regenerator, with the hidden ability Reckless. It stands 1.4 meters tall and weighs 35.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Force Palm (level 29), Drain Punch (level 33), Jump Kick (level 37), Hone Claws (level 40), U Turn (level 41), Wide Guard (level 45). It belongs to the Ground and Humanshape egg groups.

title: Druddigon
text: Druddigon is a Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Cave Pokémon. It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move. It races through narrow caves, using its sharp claws to catch prey. The skin on its face is harder than a rock. It runs through the narrow tunnels formed by Excadrill and Onix. It uses its sharp claws to catch prey. The red skin on its face is harder than rock. In narrow caves, it squares off against its enemies and charges face-first right into them. It infiltrates tunnels that Pokémon like Diglett and Dugtrio have dug and quietly waits for prey to pass through. Druddigon lives in caves, but it never skips sunbathing—it won’t be able to move if its body gets too cold. Its base stats are 77 HP, 120 Attack, 90 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 90 Special Defense, 48 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 485. It has the abilities Rough Skin and Sheer Force, with the hidden ability Mold Breaker. It stands 1.6 meters tall and weighs 139.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Crunch (level 25), Dragon Claw (level 27), Chip Away (level 31), Revenge (level 35), Night Slash (level 40), Iron Head (level 45). It belongs to the Dragon and Monster egg groups.

title: Golett
text: Golett is a Ground and Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Automaton Pokémon. The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy. These Pokémon are thought to have been created by the science of an ancient and mysterious civilization. Ancient science fashioned this Pokémon from clay. It’s been active for thousands of years. Its movements are powered by a mysterious energy. It has continued to move since ancient times, so its power may soon run out. Although ancient people apparently built it by working with clay, the source of its energy is unclear. They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders. Its base stats are 59 HP, 74 Attack, 50 Defense, 35 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 35 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 303. It has the abilities Iron Fist and Klutz, with the hidden ability No Guard. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 92.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Magnitude (level 25), Dynamic Punch (level 30), Night Shade (level 35), Shadow Ball (level 36), Curse (level 40), Heavy Slam (level 40). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Golurk
text: Golurk is a Ground and Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Automaton Pokémon. It evolves from Golett. It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control. It is said that Golurk were ordered to protect people and Pokémon by the ancient people who made them. Golurk were created to protect people and Pokémon. They run on a mysterious energy. Some say that ancient people invented Golurk to serve as a laborer. It follows its master’s orders faithfully. When the seal on its chest is removed, it rages indiscriminately, turning the whole town around it into a mountain of rubble. Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams. Its base stats are 89 HP, 124 Attack, 80 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 55 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 483. It has the abilities Iron Fist and Klutz, with the hidden ability No Guard. It stands 2.8 meters tall and weighs 330.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Stomping Tantrum (level 21), Magnitude (level 25), Dynamic Punch (level 30), Night Shade (level 35), Shadow Ball (level 36), Curse (level 40). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Pawniard
text: Pawniard is a Dark and Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Sharp Blade Pokémon. Blades comprise this Pokémon’s entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river. They fight at Bisharp’s command. They cling to their prey and inflict damage by sinking their blades into it. Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey. After shredding its prey, it sharpens its blades on a stone by the river. Each Pawniard has its own favorite sharpening stone. It follows Bisharp’s orders to a tee when it attacks enemies. After slashing an opponent, Pawniard clangs both of its blades together. It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents. Its base stats are 45 HP, 85 Attack, 70 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 340. It has the abilities Defiant and Inner Focus, with the hidden ability Pressure. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 10.2 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Assurance (level 33), Metal Sound (level 38), Embargo (level 41), Iron Defense (level 46), Night Slash (level 49), Retaliate (level 50). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Bisharp
text: Bisharp is a Dark and Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Sword Blade Pokémon. It evolves from Pawniard. It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses. Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey. This pitiless Pokémon commands a group of Pawniard to hound prey into immobility. It then moves in to finish the prey off. No matter how strong the Bisharp, it’s said that if the blade on its head is chipped, it will retire from its position as the boss. It leads a group of Pawniard. Bisharp doesn’t even change its expression when it deals the finishing blow to an opponent. It’s accompanied by a large retinue of Pawniard. Bisharp keeps a keen eye on its minions, ensuring none of them even think of double-crossing it. Its base stats are 65 HP, 125 Attack, 100 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 70 Special Defense, 70 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 490. It has the abilities Defiant and Inner Focus, with the hidden ability Pressure. It stands 1.6 meters tall and weighs 70.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Assurance (level 33), Metal Sound (level 38), Embargo (level 41), Iron Defense (level 46), Night Slash (level 49), Retaliate (level 50). It belongs to the Humanshape egg groups.

title: Bouffalant
text: Bouffalant is a Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Bash Buffalo Pokémon. Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt. They charge wildly and headbutt everything. Their headbutts have enough destructive force to derail a train. They are known to charge so wildly that if a train were to enter their territory, they would send it flying. These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd. These Pokémon live in herds of about 20 individuals. Bouffalant that betray the herd will lose the hair on their heads for some reason. Its base stats are 95 HP, 110 Attack, 95 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 95 Special Defense, 55 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 490. It has the abilities Reckless and Sap Sipper, with the hidden ability Soundproof. It stands 1.6 meters tall and weighs 94.6 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Scary Face (level 21), Revenge (level 26), Head Charge (level 31), Throat Chop (level 35), Focus Energy (level 36), Megahorn (level 41). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Rufflet
text: Rufflet is a Normal and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Eaglet Pokémon. They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is. They will challenge anything, even strong opponents, without fear. Their frequent fights help them become stronger. It stands up to massive opponents, not out of courage, but out of recklessness. But that is how it gets stronger. They pick fights indiscriminately. They grow stronger and more powerful each time they faint or are injured. With its sharp claws, this Pokémon pierces its prey, and then it pecks at them. Although it also consumes berries, it’s a carnivore at heart. With its powerful legs and sturdy claws, it can crack even the hard shells of Shellder and pluck out their insides. Its base stats are 70 HP, 83 Attack, 50 Defense, 37 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 350. It has the abilities Keen Eye and Sheer Force, with the hidden ability Hustle. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 10.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slash (level 28), Defog (level 32), Double Edge (level 34), Tailwind (level 37), Air Slash (level 41), Crush Claw (level 46). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Braviary
text: Braviary is a Normal and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Valiant Pokémon. It evolves from Rufflet. They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying. The more scars they have, the more respect these brave soldiers of the sky  get from their peers. For the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if injured. With its brave disposition, it fears nothing—not even death. Ancient Alolan people respected it, referring to it as “the hero of the sky.” It’s thought that people disturbed their habitats in the past, so Braviary banded together to fight back. The more scars it has on its front, the more heroic it’s considered to be. Those with many scars on their back are mocked by the flock. Its base stats are 100 HP, 123 Attack, 75 Defense, 57 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 80 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 510. It has the abilities Keen Eye and Sheer Force, with the hidden ability Defiant. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 41.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slash (level 28), Defog (level 32), Tailwind (level 37), Air Slash (level 41), Crush Claw (level 46), Sky Drop (level 50). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Vullaby
text: Vullaby is a Dark and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Diapered Pokémon. Their wings are too tiny to allow them to fly. They guard their posteriors with bones that were gathered by Mandibuzz. Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing. They tend to guard their posteriors with suitable bones they have found. They pursue weak Pokémon. It protects its plump posterior with the bones of prey it has consumed. As it grows, it  replaces the bones. It can’t fly yet and must wait until its wings have developed more. Since it’s still at a playful age, it hops around friskily. Mandibuzz gives it the bones it wears. Vullaby’s wings are short, so it can’t fly yet, but it jumps around, dreaming of wide open skies. Its base stats are 70 HP, 55 Attack, 75 Defense, 45 Special Attack, 65 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 370. It has the abilities Big Pecks and Overcoat, with the hidden ability Weak Armor. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 9.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Iron Defense (level 30), Defog (level 32), Tailwind (level 37), Air Slash (level 41), Dark Pulse (level 46), Embargo (level 50). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Mandibuzz
text: Mandibuzz is a Dark and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Bone Vulture Pokémon. It evolves from Vullaby. It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones. Watching from the sky, they swoop to strike weakened Pokémon on the ground. They decorate themselves with bones. They fly in circles around the sky. When they spot prey, they attack and carry it back to their nest with ease. They adorn themselves beautifully with bones. This is supposedly an effort to attract males, but no male Mandibuzz have ever been found. It circles in the sky, keeping a keen eye out for Pokémon in a weakened state. Its choicest food is Cubone. It skillfully arranges the bones of its prey to construct its nest. Most of the bones are Cubone. Its base stats are 110 HP, 65 Attack, 105 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 95 Special Defense, 80 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 510. It has the abilities Big Pecks and Overcoat, with the hidden ability Weak Armor. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 39.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Defog (level 32), Tailwind (level 37), Air Slash (level 41), Dark Pulse (level 46), Embargo (level 50), Bone Rush (level 51). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Heatmor
text: Heatmor is a Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Anteater Pokémon. It breathes through a hole in its tail while it burns with an internal fire. Durant is its prey. Using their very hot, flame-covered tongues, they burn through Durant’s steel bodies and consume their insides. It draws in air through its tail, transforms it into fire, and uses it like a tongue. It melts Durant and eats them. There’s a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill. A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides. Its base stats are 85 HP, 97 Attack, 66 Defense, 105 Special Attack, 66 Special Defense, 65 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 484. It has the abilities Gluttony and Flash Fire, with the hidden ability White Smoke. It stands 1.4 meters tall and weighs 58.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bug Bite (level 36), Slash (level 41), Fire Lash (level 44), Amnesia (level 46), Flamethrower (level 51), Stockpile (level 56). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Durant
text: Durant is a Bug and Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Iron Ant Pokémon. They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor. Durant dig nests in mountains. They build their complicated, interconnected tunnels into mazes. Individuals each play different roles in driving Heatmor, their natural predator, away from their colony. They lay their eggs deep inside their nests. When attacked by Heatmor, they retaliate using their massive mandibles. With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda. Its base stats are 58 HP, 109 Attack, 112 Defense, 48 Special Attack, 48 Special Defense, 109 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 484. It has the abilities Swarm and Hustle, with the hidden ability Truant. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 33.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Metal Claw (level 21), Bug Bite (level 26), Crunch (level 31), Iron Head (level 36), Dig (level 41), Entrainment (level 46). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Deino
text: Deino is a Dark and Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Irate Pokémon. It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous. They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds. Lacking sight, it’s unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves. When it encounters something, its first urge is usually to bite it. If it likes what it tastes, it will commit the associated scent to memory. Because it can’t see, this Pokémon is constantly biting at everything it touches, trying to keep track of its surroundings. Its base stats are 52 HP, 65 Attack, 50 Defense, 45 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 38 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 300. It has the abilities Hustle. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 17.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Crunch (level 25), Slam (level 28), Dragon Pulse (level 32), Work Up (level 38), Dragon Rush (level 42), Body Slam (level 48). It belongs to the Dragon egg groups.

title: Zweilous
text: Zweilous is a Dark and Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Hostile Pokémon. It evolves from Deino. After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along. Since their two heads do not get along and compete with each other for food, they always eat too much. The two heads do not get along. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader. While hunting for prey, Zweilous wanders its territory, its two heads often bickering over which way to go. Their two heads will fight each other over a single piece of food. Zweilous are covered in scars even without battling others. Its base stats are 72 HP, 85 Attack, 70 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 70 Special Defense, 58 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 420. It has the abilities Hustle. It stands 1.4 meters tall and weighs 50.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Crunch (level 25), Slam (level 28), Dragon Pulse (level 32), Work Up (level 38), Dragon Rush (level 42), Body Slam (level 48). It belongs to the Dragon egg groups.

title: Hydreigon
text: Hydreigon is a Dark and Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Brutal Pokémon. It evolves from Zweilous. This brutal Pokémon travels the skies on its six wings. Anything that moves seems like a foe to it, triggering its attack. The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything. It responds to movement by attacking. This scary, three-headed Pokémon devours everything in its path! There are a slew of stories about villages that were destroyed by Hydreigon. It bites anything that moves. The three heads take turns sinking their teeth into the opponent. Their attacks won’t slow until their target goes down. Its base stats are 92 HP, 105 Attack, 90 Defense, 125 Special Attack, 90 Special Defense, 98 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 600. It has the abilities Levitate. It stands 1.8 meters tall and weighs 160.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slam (level 28), Dragon Pulse (level 32), Work Up (level 38), Dragon Rush (level 42), Body Slam (level 48), Scary Face (level 55). It belongs to the Dragon egg groups.

title: Larvesta
text: Larvesta is a Bug and Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Torch Pokémon. This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames. The base of volcanoes is where they make their homes. They shoot fire from their five horns to repel attacking enemies. Said to have been born from the sun, it spews fire from its horns and encases itself in a cocoon of fire when it evolves. When battling opponents, it sprays fire from its five horns. The max temperature of the flames can reach nearly 5,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It protects itself with flame. Long years ago, people believed Larvesta had a nest on the sun. The people of ancient times believed that Larvesta fell from the sun. Its base stats are 55 HP, 85 Attack, 55 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 55 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 360. It has the abilities Flame Body, with the hidden ability Swarm. It stands 1.1 meters tall and weighs 28.8 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Take Down (level 20), Screech (level 30), Flame Charge (level 30), Bug Bite (level 40), Double Edge (level 50), Flame Wheel (level 60). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Volcarona
text: Volcarona is a Bug and Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Sun Pokémon. It evolves from Larvesta. When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona’s fire provided a replacement for the sun. A sea of fire engulfs the surroundings of their battles, since they use their six wings to scatter their ember scales. Thought to be an embodiment of the sun, it appeared during a bitterly cold winter and saved Pokémon from freezing. As it flies, it scatters its flaming scales. It was feared by ancient people, who referred to it as “the rage of the sun.” According to legends, it was hatched from a flaming cocoon to save people and Pokémon that were suffering from the cold. Volcarona scatters burning scales. Some say it does this to start fires. Others say it’s trying to rescue those that suffer in the cold. Its base stats are 85 HP, 60 Attack, 65 Defense, 135 Special Attack, 105 Special Defense, 100 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 550. It has the abilities Flame Body, with the hidden ability Swarm. It stands 1.6 meters tall and weighs 46.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Screech (level 30), Whirlwind (level 40), Silver Wind (level 50), Quiver Dance (level 59), Heat Wave (level 60), Fire Blast (level 70). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Cobalion
text: Cobalion is a Steel and Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Iron Will Pokémon. This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed. It has a body and heart of steel. Its glare is sufficient to make even an unruly Pokémon obey it. It has a body and heart of steel. It worked with its allies to punish people when they hurt Pokémon. This Pokémon appears in a legend alongside Terrakion and Virizion, fighting against humans in defense of the Unova region’s Pokémon. From the moment it’s born, this Pokémon radiates the air of a leader. Its presence will calm even vicious foes. Its base stats are 91 HP, 90 Attack, 129 Defense, 90 Special Attack, 72 Special Defense, 108 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 580. It has the abilities Justified. It stands 2.1 meters tall and weighs 250.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Take Down (level 19), Helping Hand (level 25), Retaliate (level 31), Iron Head (level 37), Sacred Sword (level 42), Swords Dance (level 49). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Terrakion
text: Terrakion is a Rock and Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Cavern Pokémon. This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans. Its charge is strong enough to break through a giant castle wall in one blow. This Pokémon is spoken of in legends. Spoken of in legend, this Pokémon used its phenomenal power to destroy a castle in its effort to protect Pokémon. It has phenomenal power. It will mercilessly crush anyone or anything that bullies small Pokémon. In Unovan legend, Terrakion battled against humans in an effort to protect other Pokémon. Its base stats are 91 HP, 129 Attack, 90 Defense, 72 Special Attack, 90 Special Defense, 108 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 580. It has the abilities Justified. It stands 1.9 meters tall and weighs 260.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Take Down (level 19), Helping Hand (level 25), Retaliate (level 31), Rock Slide (level 37), Sacred Sword (level 42), Swords Dance (level 49). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Virizion
text: Virizion is a Grass and Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Grassland Pokémon. This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down. Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents. Legends say this Pokémon confounded opponents with its swift movements. A legend tells of this Pokémon working together with Cobalion and Terrakion to protect the Pokémon of the Unova region. It darts around opponents with a flurry of quick movements, slicing them up with its horns. Its base stats are 91 HP, 90 Attack, 72 Defense, 90 Special Attack, 129 Special Defense, 108 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 580. It has the abilities Justified. It stands 2.0 meters tall and weighs 200.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Helping Hand (level 25), Retaliate (level 31), Giga Drain (level 37), Synthesis (level 42), Sacred Sword (level 42), Swords Dance (level 49). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Tornadus incarnate
text: Tornadus incarnate is a Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Cyclone Pokémon. The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph. Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power is great enough to blow houses away. In every direction it flies, creating winds so powerful, they blow everything away. This storm-stirring Pokémon is said to cause the seasons to turn by whipping up the air. I suspect its humanlike form to be a false one. Its base stats are 79 HP, 115 Attack, 70 Defense, 125 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 111 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 580. It has the abilities Prankster, with the hidden ability Defiant. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 63.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Agility (level 37), Air Slash (level 43), Crunch (level 49), Bleakwind Storm (level 52), Tailwind (level 55), Rain Dance (level 61). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Thundurus incarnate
text: Thundurus incarnate is a Electric and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Bolt Strike Pokémon. Countless charred remains mar the landscape of places through which Thundurus has passed. The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts. As it flies around, it shoots lightning all over the place and causes forest fires. It is therefore disliked. They say this wielder of electricity has waged war with its nemesis, Tornadus, since time immemorial. The lightning bolts it hurls pierce the very earth and enrich the soil. Its base stats are 79 HP, 115 Attack, 70 Defense, 125 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 111 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 580. It has the abilities Prankster, with the hidden ability Defiant. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 61.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Discharge (level 43), Crunch (level 49), Wildbolt Storm (level 52), Charge (level 55), Rain Dance (level 60), Nasty Plot (level 61). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Reshiram
text: Reshiram is a Dragon and Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Vast White Pokémon. This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it. When Reshiram’s tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world’s weather. This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth. Flames spew from its tail as it flies through the sky like a jet airplane. It’s said that this Pokémon will scorch the world. According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down. Its base stats are 100 HP, 120 Attack, 100 Defense, 150 Special Attack, 120 Special Defense, 90 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 680. It has the abilities Turboblaze. It stands 3.2 meters tall and weighs 330.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slash (level 36), Extrasensory (level 43), Fusion Flare (level 50), Dragon Pulse (level 54), Noble Roar (level 64), Crunch (level 71). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Zekrom
text: Zekrom is a Dragon and Electric type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Deep Black Pokémon. Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail. This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity. This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with lightning. It assists those who want to build an ideal world. When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings. Mythology tells us that if people lose the righteousness in their hearts, their kingdoms will be razed by Zekrom’s lightning. Its base stats are 100 HP, 150 Attack, 120 Defense, 120 Special Attack, 100 Special Defense, 90 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 680. It has the abilities Teravolt. It stands 2.9 meters tall and weighs 345.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slash (level 36), Zen Headbutt (level 43), Fusion Bolt (level 50), Dragon Claw (level 54), Noble Roar (level 64), Crunch (level 71). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Landorus incarnate
text: Landorus incarnate is a Ground and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Abundance Pokémon. Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.” The energy that comes pouring from its tail increases the nutrition in the soil, making crops grow to great size. From the forces of lightning and wind, it creates energy to give nutrients to the soil and make the land abundant. When the incarnations of wind and of lightning clash, Landorus arrives to quell the conflict. After the tempests and thunderbolts abate, the land is sure to be blessed with bountiful harvests. Its base stats are 89 HP, 125 Attack, 90 Defense, 115 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 101 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 600. It has the abilities Sand Force, with the hidden ability Sheer Force. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 68.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Crunch (level 41), Earth Power (level 43), Rock Slide (level 49), Sandsear Storm (level 52), Earthquake (level 55), Sandstorm (level 61). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Kyurem
text: Kyurem is a Dragon and Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Boundary Pokémon. It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out. It can produce ultracold air. Its body is frozen. This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals. Dwelling within it is a power even greater than that of Reshiram or Zekrom, but the extreme cold keeps that power bound. It appears that this Pokémon uses its powers over ice to freeze its own body in order to stabilize its cellular structure. Its base stats are 125 HP, 130 Attack, 90 Defense, 130 Special Attack, 90 Special Defense, 95 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 660. It has the abilities Pressure. It stands 3.0 meters tall and weighs 325.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slash (level 36), Scary Face (level 43), Glaciate (level 50), Dragon Pulse (level 57), Noble Roar (level 64), Endeavor (level 71). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Keldeo ordinary
text: Keldeo ordinary is a Water and Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Colt Pokémon. By blasting water from its hooves, it can glide across water. It excels at using leg moves while battling. It crosses the world, running over the surfaces of oceans and rivers. It appears at scenic waterfronts. When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow. Cobalion, Terrakion, and Virizion taught this Pokémon how to fight. It dashes across the world, seeking more opportunities to further its training. They say that Keldeo must survive harsh battles and fully develop the horn on its forehead before this Pokémon’s true power will awaken. Its base stats are 91 HP, 72 Attack, 90 Defense, 129 Special Attack, 90 Special Defense, 108 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 580. It has the abilities Justified. It stands 1.4 meters tall and weighs 48.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Take Down (level 19), Helping Hand (level 25), Retaliate (level 31), Aqua Tail (level 37), Sacred Sword (level 43), Swords Dance (level 49). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Meloetta aria
text: Meloetta aria is a Normal and Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Melody Pokémon. Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it. Many famous songs have been inspired by the melodies that Meloetta plays. The melodies sung by Meloetta have the power to make Pokémon that hear them happy or sad. Its base stats are 100 HP, 77 Attack, 77 Defense, 128 Special Attack, 128 Special Defense, 90 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 600. It has the abilities Serene Grace. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 6.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Psybeam (level 31), Echoed Voice (level 36), U Turn (level 43), Wake Up Slap (level 50), Relic Song (level 50), Psychic (level 57). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Genesect
text: Genesect is a Bug and Steel type Pokemon introduced in Generation 5. It is known as the Paleozoic Pokémon. Over 300 million years ago, it was feared as the strongest of hunters. It has been modified by Team Plasma. This ancient bug Pokémon was altered by Team Plasma. They upgraded the cannon on its back. This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back. Its base stats are 71 HP, 120 Attack, 95 Defense, 120 Special Attack, 95 Special Defense, 99 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 600. It has the abilities Download. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 82.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slash (level 29), Metal Sound (level 33), Signal Beam (level 40), Tri Attack (level 44), X Scissor (level 51), Bug Buzz (level 55). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Chespin
text: Chespin is a Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Spiny Nut Pokémon. The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock. Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn’t faze it. Its base stats are 56 HP, 61 Attack, 65 Defense, 48 Special Attack, 45 Special Defense, 38 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 313. It has the abilities Overgrow, with the hidden ability Bulletproof. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 9.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bite (level 11), Leech Seed (level 15), Pin Missile (level 18), Take Down (level 27), Seed Bomb (level 32), Mud Shot (level 35). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Quilladin
text: Quilladin is a Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Spiny Armor Pokémon. It evolves from Chespin. It relies on its sturdy shell to deflect predators’ attacks. It counterattacks with its sharp quills. They strengthen their lower bodies by running into one another. They are very kind and won’t start fights. Its base stats are 61 HP, 78 Attack, 95 Defense, 56 Special Attack, 58 Special Defense, 57 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 405. It has the abilities Overgrow, with the hidden ability Bulletproof. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 29.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Leech Seed (level 15), Pin Missile (level 20), Needle Arm (level 26), Take Down (level 30), Seed Bomb (level 35), Mud Shot (level 39). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Chesnaught
text: Chesnaught is a Grass and Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Spiny Armor Pokémon. It evolves from Quilladin. Its Tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body. When it takes a defensive posture with its fists guarding its face, it could withstand a bomb blast. Its base stats are 88 HP, 107 Attack, 122 Defense, 74 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 64 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 530. It has the abilities Overgrow, with the hidden ability Bulletproof. It stands 1.6 meters tall and weighs 90.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Needle Arm (level 26), Take Down (level 30), Seed Bomb (level 35), Spiky Shield (level 36), Mud Shot (level 41), Bulk Up (level 44). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Fennekin
text: Fennekin is a Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Fox Pokémon. Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit. As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears. Its base stats are 40 HP, 45 Attack, 40 Defense, 62 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 307. It has the abilities Blaze, with the hidden ability Magician. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 9.4 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Fire Spin (level 20), Lucky Chant (level 25), Light Screen (level 27), Psyshock (level 31), Flamethrower (level 35), Will O Wisp (level 38). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Braixen
text: Braixen is a Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Fox Pokémon. It evolves from Fennekin. It has a twig stuck in its tail. With friction from its tail fur, it sets the twig on fire and launches into battle. When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies. Its base stats are 59 HP, 59 Attack, 58 Defense, 90 Special Attack, 70 Special Defense, 73 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 409. It has the abilities Blaze, with the hidden ability Magician. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 14.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Fire Spin (level 22), Lucky Chant (level 27), Light Screen (level 30), Psyshock (level 34), Flamethrower (level 41), Will O Wisp (level 45). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Delphox
text: Delphox is a Fire and Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Fox Pokémon. It evolves from Braixen. It gazes into the flame at the tip of its branch to achieve a focused state, which allows it to see into the future. Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame. Its base stats are 75 HP, 69 Attack, 72 Defense, 114 Special Attack, 100 Special Defense, 104 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 534. It has the abilities Blaze, with the hidden ability Magician. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 39.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Lucky Chant (level 27), Light Screen (level 30), Psyshock (level 34), Mystical Fire (level 36), Flamethrower (level 42), Will O Wisp (level 47). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Froakie
text: Froakie is a Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Bubble Frog Pokémon. It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked. It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings. Its base stats are 41 HP, 56 Attack, 40 Defense, 62 Special Attack, 44 Special Defense, 71 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 314. It has the abilities Torrent, with the hidden ability Protean. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 7.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Lick (level 10), Water Pulse (level 14), Smokescreen (level 18), Round (level 21), Fling (level 25), Smack Down (level 29). It belongs to the Water1 egg groups.

title: Frogadier
text: Frogadier is a Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Bubble Frog Pokémon. It evolves from Froakie. It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away. Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time. Its base stats are 54 HP, 63 Attack, 52 Defense, 83 Special Attack, 56 Special Defense, 97 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 405. It has the abilities Torrent, with the hidden ability Protean. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 10.9 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Lick (level 10), Water Pulse (level 14), Smokescreen (level 20), Round (level 23), Fling (level 28), Smack Down (level 33). It belongs to the Water1 egg groups.

title: Greninja
text: Greninja is a Water and Dark type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Ninja Pokémon. It evolves from Frogadier. It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two. It appears and vanishes with a ninja’s grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water. Its base stats are 72 HP, 95 Attack, 67 Defense, 103 Special Attack, 71 Special Defense, 122 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 530. It has the abilities Torrent, with the hidden ability Protean. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 40.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Shadow Sneak (level 23), Spikes (level 28), Feint Attack (level 33), Aerial Ace (level 33), Water Shuriken (level 36), Substitute (level 43). It belongs to the Water1 egg groups.

title: Bunnelby
text: Bunnelby is a Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Digging Pokémon. They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through. It has ears like shovels. Digging holes strengthens its ears so much that they can sever thick roots effortlessly. It excels at digging holes. Using its ears, it can dig a nest 33 feet deep in one night. It’s very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight’s flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments. Its base stats are 38 HP, 36 Attack, 38 Defense, 32 Special Attack, 36 Special Defense, 57 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 237. It has the abilities Pickup and Cheek Pouch, with the hidden ability Huge Power. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 5.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Double Kick (level 20), Bulldoze (level 21), Odor Sleuth (level 25), Flail (level 29), Swords Dance (level 33), Dig (level 33). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Diggersby
text: Diggersby is a Normal and Ground type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Digging Pokémon. It evolves from Bunnelby. With their powerful ears, they can heft boulders of a ton or more with ease. They can be a big help at construction sites. As powerful as an excavator, its ears can reduce dense bedrock to rubble. When it’s finished digging, it lounges lazily. With power equal to an excavator, it can dig through dense bedrock. It’s a huge help during tunnel construction. The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon. Its base stats are 85 HP, 56 Attack, 77 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 77 Special Defense, 78 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 423. It has the abilities Pickup and Cheek Pouch, with the hidden ability Huge Power. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 42.4 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Take Down (level 15), Mud Shot (level 18), Double Kick (level 20), Odor Sleuth (level 26), Flail (level 31), Dig (level 37). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Fletchling
text: Fletchling is a Normal and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Tiny Robin Pokémon. These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements. Despite the beauty of its lilting voice, it’s merciless to intruders that enter its territory. This amiable Pokémon is easy to train. But when battle is joined, it shows its ferocious side. When it’s excited, its temperature can double, spiking hormone production in its body. While it’s an amiable Pokémon, if you touch it unexpectedly, it will heat up its body in an instant, and you’ll be burned. Its body is always warm. Trainers who live in cold areas apparently sleep with it in their bed.
