Its base stats are 45 HP, 50 Attack, 43 Defense, 40 Special Attack, 38 Special Defense, 62 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 278. It has the abilities Big Pecks, with the hidden ability Gale Wings. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 1.7 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Roost (level 21), Razor Wind (level 25), Natural Gift (level 29), Aerial Ace (level 30), Flame Charge (level 34), Acrobatics (level 39). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Fletchinder
text: Fletchinder is a Fire and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Ember Pokémon. It evolves from Fletchling. From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass. The hotter the flame sac on its belly, the faster it can fly, but it takes some time to get the fire going. From its beak, it fires embers at its prey. Once it has caught them, it grills them at high heat before feasting upon them. It will not tolerate other Fletchinder entering its territory, which has a radius of several miles. It scatters embers in the tall grass. Then bug Pokémon, frightened by the flames, come flying out, and Fletchinder gobbles them up. Its speed right after takeoff already puts it in the top speed class of all bird Pokémon. Its base stats are 62 HP, 73 Attack, 55 Defense, 56 Special Attack, 52 Special Defense, 84 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 382. It has the abilities Flame Body, with the hidden ability Gale Wings. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 16.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Roost (level 25), Razor Wind (level 27), Natural Gift (level 31), Aerial Ace (level 36), Flame Charge (level 38), Acrobatics (level 42). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Talonflame
text: Talonflame is a Fire and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Scorching Pokémon. It evolves from Fletchinder. In the fever of an exciting battle, it showers embers from the gaps between its feathers and takes to the air. When attacking prey, it can reach speeds of up to 310 mph. It finishes its prey off with a colossal kick. Its favorite foods are Wingull and Pikipek. It attacks with a powerful kick and grasps them firmly in its talons. It zooms directly at its prey at flight speeds of close to 310 mph, while fiery embers scatter from gaps in its feathers. Its tough wings don’t allow fire to pass through them. A long time ago, firefighters’ outfits were made from Talonflame wings. Bird Pokémon make up most of its diet. It approaches at high speeds and smacks them down to the ground with its powerful kick. Its base stats are 78 HP, 81 Attack, 71 Defense, 74 Special Attack, 69 Special Defense, 126 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 499. It has the abilities Flame Body, with the hidden ability Gale Wings. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 24.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Roost (level 25), Razor Wind (level 27), Natural Gift (level 31), Aerial Ace (level 38), Flame Charge (level 39), Acrobatics (level 44). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Scatterbug
text: Scatterbug is a Bug type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Scatterdust Pokémon. When under attack from bird Pokémon, it spews a poisonous black powder that causes paralysis on contact. The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate. Its base stats are 38 HP, 35 Attack, 40 Defense, 27 Special Attack, 25 Special Defense, 35 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 200. It has the abilities Shield Dust and Compound Eyes, with the hidden ability Friend Guard. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 2.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Tackle (level 1), String Shot (level 1), Stun Spore (level 6), Bug Bite (level 15). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Spewpa
text: Spewpa is a Bug type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Scatterdust Pokémon. It evolves from Scatterbug. It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it quickly bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them. The beaks of bird Pokémon can’t begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder. Its base stats are 45 HP, 22 Attack, 60 Defense, 27 Special Attack, 30 Special Defense, 29 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 213. It has the abilities Shed Skin, with the hidden ability Friend Guard. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 8.4 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Harden (level 1), Protect (level 9). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Vivillon
text: Vivillon is a Bug and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Scale Pokémon. It evolves from Spewpa. Vivillon with many different patterns are found all over the world. These patterns are affected by the climate of their habitat. The patterns on this Pokémon’s wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales. Its base stats are 80 HP, 52 Attack, 50 Defense, 90 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 89 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 411. It has the abilities Shield Dust and Compound Eyes, with the hidden ability Friend Guard. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 17.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Powder (level 1), Struggle Bug (level 12), Psybeam (level 17), Supersonic (level 21), Draining Kiss (level 25), Aromatherapy (level 31). It belongs to the Bug egg groups.

title: Litleo
text: Litleo is a Fire and Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Lion Cub Pokémon. The stronger the opponent it faces, the more heat surges from its mane and the more power flows through its body. They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot-blooded Pokémon are quick to fight. When they’re young, they live with a pride. Once they’re able to hunt prey on their own, they’re kicked out and have to make their own way. This hot-blooded Pokémon is filled with curiosity. When it gets angry or starts fighting, its short mane gets hot. Its base stats are 62 HP, 50 Attack, 58 Defense, 73 Special Attack, 54 Special Defense, 72 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 369. It has the abilities Rivalry and Unnerve, with the hidden ability Moxie. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 13.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Noble Roar (level 15), Take Down (level 20), Fire Fang (level 23), Endeavor (level 28), Echoed Voice (level 33), Flamethrower (level 36). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Pyroar male
text: Pyroar male is a Fire and Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Royal Pokémon. It evolves from Litleo. The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride. With fiery breath of more than 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, they viciously threaten any challenger. The females protect the pride’s cubs. The males are usually lazy, but when attacked by a strong foe, a male will protect its friends with no regard for its own safety. The temperature of its breath is over 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, but Pyroar doesn’t use it on its prey. This Pokémon prefers to eat raw meat. Its base stats are 86 HP, 68 Attack, 72 Defense, 109 Special Attack, 66 Special Defense, 106 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 507. It has the abilities Rivalry and Unnerve, with the hidden ability Moxie. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 81.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Noble Roar (level 15), Take Down (level 20), Fire Fang (level 23), Endeavor (level 28), Echoed Voice (level 33), Flamethrower (level 38). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Flabebe
text: Flabebe is a Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Single Bloom Pokémon. It draws out and controls the hidden power of flowers. The flower Flabébé holds is most likely part of its body. When it finds a flower it likes, it dwells on that flower its whole life long. It floats in the wind’s embrace with an untroubled heart. It’s not safe without the power of a flower, but it will keep traveling around until it finds one with the color and shape it wants. Flabébé wears a crown made from pollen it’s collected from its flower. The crown has hidden healing properties. Its base stats are 44 HP, 38 Attack, 39 Defense, 61 Special Attack, 79 Special Defense, 42 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 303. It has the abilities Flower Veil, with the hidden ability Symbiosis. It stands 0.1 meters tall and weighs 0.1 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Wish (level 20), Magical Leaf (level 22), Grassy Terrain (level 24), Petal Blizzard (level 28), Synthesis (level 33), Aromatherapy (level 33). It belongs to the Fairy egg groups.

title: Floette
text: Floette is a Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Single Bloom Pokémon. It evolves from Flabebe. It flutters around fields of flowers and cares for flowers that are starting to wilt. It draws out the hidden power of flowers to battle. When the flowers of a well-tended flower bed bloom, it appears and celebrates with an elegant dance. It raises flowers and uses them as weapons. The more gorgeous the blossom, the more power it contains. It gives its own power to flowers, pouring its heart into caring for them. Floette never forgives anyone who messes up a flower bed. Its base stats are 54 HP, 45 Attack, 47 Defense, 75 Special Attack, 98 Special Defense, 52 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 371. It has the abilities Flower Veil, with the hidden ability Symbiosis. It stands 0.2 meters tall and weighs 0.9 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Wish (level 20), Magical Leaf (level 25), Grassy Terrain (level 27), Petal Blizzard (level 33), Synthesis (level 38), Aromatherapy (level 38). It belongs to the Fairy egg groups.

title: Florges
text: Florges is a Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Garden Pokémon. It evolves from Floette. It claims exquisite flower gardens as its territory, and it obtains power from basking in the energy emitted by flowering plants. In times long past, governors of castles would invite Florges to create flower gardens to embellish the castle domains. It controls the flowers it grows. The petal blizzards that Florges triggers are overwhelming in their beauty and power. Its life can span several hundred years. It’s said to devote its entire life to protecting gardens. Its base stats are 78 HP, 65 Attack, 68 Defense, 112 Special Attack, 154 Special Defense, 75 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 552. It has the abilities Flower Veil, with the hidden ability Symbiosis. It stands 1.1 meters tall and weighs 10.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Aromatherapy (level 1), Magical Leaf (level 1), Lucky Chant (level 1), Grass Knot (level 1), Petal Blizzard (level 1), Disarming Voice (level 1). It belongs to the Fairy egg groups.

title: Skiddo
text: Skiddo is a Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Mount Pokémon. Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition. If it has sunshine and water, it doesn’t need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back. Its base stats are 66 HP, 65 Attack, 48 Defense, 62 Special Attack, 57 Special Defense, 52 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 350. It has the abilities Sap Sipper, with the hidden ability Grass Pelt. It stands 0.9 meters tall and weighs 31.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Worry Seed (level 16), Synthesis (level 20), Take Down (level 22), Bulldoze (level 26), Seed Bomb (level 30), Bulk Up (level 34). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Gogoat
text: Gogoat is a Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Mount Pokémon. It evolves from Skiddo. It can tell how its Trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathic sense lets them run as if one being. They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns. Its base stats are 123 HP, 100 Attack, 62 Defense, 97 Special Attack, 81 Special Defense, 68 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 531. It has the abilities Sap Sipper, with the hidden ability Grass Pelt. It stands 1.7 meters tall and weighs 91.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Synthesis (level 20), Take Down (level 22), Bulldoze (level 26), Seed Bomb (level 30), Bulk Up (level 34), Double Edge (level 40). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Pancham
text: Pancham is a Fighting type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Playful Pokémon. It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark. It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can’t help grinning if anyone pats its head. It grows up imitating the behavior of Pangoro, which it looks up to as a leader. There’s no point to the leaf in its mouth, aside from an effort to look cool. It’s mischievous, so it’s not well suited to inexperienced Trainers. It follows Pangoro around like a henchman. When Pancham makes a big mistake, its leaf gets taken away. It’s desperate to intimidate its opponents. Be nice and pretend to be scared if you catch it glaring at you intensely. Its base stats are 67 HP, 82 Attack, 62 Defense, 46 Special Attack, 48 Special Defense, 43 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 348. It has the abilities Iron Fist and Mold Breaker, with the hidden ability Scrappy. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 8.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Comet Punch (level 15), Low Sweep (level 16), Slash (level 20), Circle Throw (level 25), Vital Throw (level 27), Body Slam (level 33). It belongs to the Ground and Humanshape egg groups.

title: Pangoro
text: Pangoro is a Fighting and Dark type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Daunting Pokémon. It evolves from Pancham. Although it possesses a violent temperament, it won’t put up with bullying. It uses the leaf in its mouth to sense the movements of its enemies. It charges ahead and bashes its opponents like a berserker, uncaring about any hits it might take. Its arms are mighty enough to snap a telephone pole. It boasts superb physical strength. Those who wish to become Pangoro’s Trainer have no choice but to converse with their fists. From the slight twitches of its bamboo leaf, it deduces its opponent’s movements. It’s eager to tussle but kindhearted toward its companions. This rowdy Pokémon boasts great physical strength. Many Trainers are also smitten by its lively character. Pangoro is the type to talk with its fists. It doesn’t say much as it pummels opponents with enough force to send a dump truck flying. Its base stats are 95 HP, 124 Attack, 78 Defense, 69 Special Attack, 71 Special Defense, 58 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 495. It has the abilities Iron Fist and Mold Breaker, with the hidden ability Scrappy. It stands 2.1 meters tall and weighs 136.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Comet Punch (level 15), Slash (level 20), Circle Throw (level 25), Vital Throw (level 27), Body Slam (level 35), Crunch (level 42). It belongs to the Ground and Humanshape egg groups.

title: Furfrou
text: Furfrou is a Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Poodle Pokémon. Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements. Historically, in the Kalos region, these Pokémon were the designated guardians of the king. There was an era when aristocrats would compete to see who could trim their Furfrou’s fur into the most exquisite style. Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it. Its base stats are 75 HP, 80 Attack, 60 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 90 Special Defense, 102 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 472. It has the abilities Fur Coat. It stands 1.2 meters tall and weighs 28.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Baby Doll Eyes (level 9), Headbutt (level 12), Tail Whip (level 15), Bite (level 22), Odor Sleuth (level 27), Retaliate (level 33). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Espurr
text: Espurr is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Restraint Pokémon. The organ that emits its intense psychic power is sheltered by its ears to keep power from leaking out. It has enough psychic energy to blast everything within 300 feet of itself, but it has no control over its power. Though Espurr’s expression never changes, behind that blank stare is an intense struggle to contain its devastating psychic power. There’s enough psychic power in Espurr to send a wrestler flying, but because this power can’t be controlled, Espurr finds it troublesome. Its base stats are 62 HP, 48 Attack, 54 Defense, 63 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 68 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 355. It has the abilities Keen Eye and Infiltrator, with the hidden ability Own Tempo. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 3.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Scratch (level 1), Leer (level 1), Covet (level 5), Confusion (level 9), Light Screen (level 13), Psybeam (level 17). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Meowstic male
text: Meowstic male is a Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Constraint Pokémon. It evolves from Espurr. When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck into dust. The eyeball patterns on the interior of its ears emit psychic energy. It keeps the patterns tightly covered because that power is too immense. Revealing the eyelike patterns on the insides of its ears will unleash its psychic powers. It normally keeps the patterns hidden, however. The defensive instinct of the males is strong. It’s when they’re protecting themselves or their partners that they unleash their full power. Its base stats are 74 HP, 48 Attack, 76 Defense, 83 Special Attack, 81 Special Defense, 104 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 466. It has the abilities Keen Eye and Infiltrator, with the hidden ability Prankster. It stands 0.6 meters tall and weighs 8.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Disarming Voice (level 22), Psyshock (level 25), Charm (level 28), Miracle Eye (level 31), Reflect (level 35), Psychic (level 40). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Honedge
text: Honedge is a Steel and Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Sword Pokémon. Apparently this Pokémon is born when a departed spirit inhabits a sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force. If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person’s arm and drains that person’s life energy completely. Honedge’s soul once belonged to a person who was killed a long time ago by the sword that makes up Honedge’s body. The blue eye on the sword’s handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people’s lives away. Its base stats are 45 HP, 80 Attack, 100 Defense, 35 Special Attack, 37 Special Defense, 28 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 325. It has the abilities No Guard. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 2.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Autotomize (level 18), Shadow Sneak (level 20), Aerial Ace (level 22), Retaliate (level 26), Slash (level 29), Iron Defense (level 32). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Doublade
text: Doublade is a Steel and Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Sword Pokémon. It evolves from Honedge. When Honedge evolves, it divides into two swords, which cooperate via telepathy to coordinate attacks and slash their enemies to ribbons. The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even for an opponent greatly accomplished at swordplay. Honedge evolves into twins. The two blades rub together to emit a metallic sound that unnerves opponents. The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey. Its base stats are 59 HP, 110 Attack, 150 Defense, 45 Special Attack, 49 Special Defense, 35 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 448. It has the abilities No Guard. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 4.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Autotomize (level 18), Shadow Sneak (level 20), Aerial Ace (level 22), Retaliate (level 26), Slash (level 29), Iron Defense (level 32). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Aegislash shield
text: Aegislash shield is a Steel and Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Royal Sword Pokémon. It evolves from Doublade. Generations of kings were attended by these Pokémon, which used their spectral power to manipulate and control people and Pokémon. Apparently, it can detect the innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king. In this defensive stance, Aegislash uses its steel body and a force field of spectral power to reduce the damage of any attack. Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking. Its base stats are 60 HP, 50 Attack, 140 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 140 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 500. It has the abilities Stance Change. It stands 1.7 meters tall and weighs 53.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Iron Defense (level 1), Power Trick (level 1), Night Slash (level 1), Shadow Sneak (level 1), Iron Head (level 1), Head Smash (level 1). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Spritzee
text: Spritzee is a Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Perfume Pokémon. It emits a scent that enraptures those who smell it. This fragrance changes depending on what it has eaten. In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked. A scent pouch within this Pokémon’s body allows it to create various scents. A change in its diet will alter the fragrance it produces. The scent its body gives off enraptures those who smell it. Noble ladies had no shortage of love for Spritzee. Its base stats are 78 HP, 52 Attack, 60 Defense, 63 Special Attack, 65 Special Defense, 23 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 341. It has the abilities Healer, with the hidden ability Aroma Veil. It stands 0.2 meters tall and weighs 0.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Draining Kiss (level 21), Aromatherapy (level 25), Attract (level 29), Moonblast (level 31), Charm (level 35), Flail (level 38). It belongs to the Fairy egg groups.

title: Aromatisse
text: Aromatisse is a Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Fragrance Pokémon. It evolves from Spritzee. It devises various scents, pleasant and unpleasant, and emits scents that its enemies dislike in order to gain an edge in battle. Its scent is so overpowering that, unless a Trainer happens to really enjoy the smell, he or she will have a hard time walking alongside it. The scent that constantly emits from its fur is so powerful that this Pokémon’s companions will eventually lose their sense of smell. The scents Aromatisse can produce range from sweet smells that bolster allies to foul smells that sap an opponent’s will to fight. Its base stats are 101 HP, 72 Attack, 72 Defense, 99 Special Attack, 89 Special Defense, 29 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 462. It has the abilities Healer, with the hidden ability Aroma Veil. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 15.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Attract (level 29), Moonblast (level 31), Charm (level 35), Flail (level 38), Misty Terrain (level 42), Skill Swap (level 44). It belongs to the Fairy egg groups.

title: Swirlix
text: Swirlix is a Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Cotton Candy Pokémon. To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and sticky as cotton candy. Because it eats nothing but sweets, its fur is as sticky sweet as cotton candy. It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn’t get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy. The sweet smell of cotton candy perfumes Swirlix’s fluffy fur. This Pokémon spits out sticky string to tangle up its enemies. Its base stats are 62 HP, 48 Attack, 66 Defense, 59 Special Attack, 57 Special Defense, 49 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 341. It has the abilities Sweet Veil, with the hidden ability Unburden. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 3.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include String Shot (level 21), Endeavor (level 21), Aromatherapy (level 26), Draining Kiss (level 31), Energy Ball (level 36), Cotton Guard (level 41). It belongs to the Fairy egg groups.

title: Slurpuff
text: Slurpuff is a Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Meringue Pokémon. It evolves from Swirlix. It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work. Its sense of smell is 100 million times better than a human’s, so even the faintest scent tells it about everything in the area. It’s like it can see with its nose! By taking in a person’s scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It’s hoped that this skill will have many medical applications. Slurpuff’s fur contains a lot of air, making it soft to the touch and lighter than it looks. Its base stats are 82 HP, 80 Attack, 86 Defense, 85 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 72 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 480. It has the abilities Sweet Veil, with the hidden ability Unburden. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 5.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Endeavor (level 21), Aromatherapy (level 26), Draining Kiss (level 31), Energy Ball (level 36), Cotton Guard (level 41), Sticky Web (level 42). It belongs to the Fairy egg groups.

title: Inkay
text: Inkay is a Dark and Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Revolving Pokémon. Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight. It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent’s will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide. It draws prey near with its blinking lights and then wraps them up in its long tentacles and holds them in place. When exchanging information with others of its kind, it flashes the light-emitting spots on its body in a complex rhythm. It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light. By exposing foes to the blinking of its luminescent spots, Inkay demoralizes them, and then it seizes the chance to flee. Its base stats are 53 HP, 54 Attack, 53 Defense, 37 Special Attack, 46 Special Defense, 45 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 288. It has the abilities Contrary and Suction Cups, with the hidden ability Infiltrator. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 3.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Hypnosis (level 18), Psybeam (level 21), Switcheroo (level 23), Payback (level 27), Light Screen (level 31), Pluck (level 35). It belongs to the Water1 and Water2 egg groups.

title: Malamar
text: Malamar is a Dark and Psychic type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Overturning Pokémon. It evolves from Inkay. It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants. It lures its prey close with hypnotic motions, then wraps its tentacles around it before finishing it off with digestive fluids. When it comes to strong hypnosis, there’s an endless number of people who utilize Malamar for their nefarious deeds. It flashes the pattern on its torso to control its prey and draw them in. The edges of its fins are supremely sharp. Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar’s control. It’s said that Malamar’s hypnotic powers played a role in certain history-changing events. Its base stats are 86 HP, 92 Attack, 88 Defense, 68 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 73 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 482. It has the abilities Contrary and Suction Cups, with the hidden ability Infiltrator. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 47.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Topsy Turvy (level 15), Hypnosis (level 18), Psybeam (level 21), Switcheroo (level 23), Payback (level 27), Light Screen (level 31). It belongs to the Water1 and Water2 egg groups.

title: Binacle
text: Binacle is a Rock and Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Two-Handed Pokémon. Two Binacle live together on one rock. When they fight, one of them will move to a different rock. They stretch and then contract, yanking their rocks along with them in bold hops. They eat seaweed that washes up on the shoreline. After two Binacle find a suitably sized rock, they adhere themselves to it and live together. They cooperate to gather food during high tide. If the two don’t work well together, both their offense and defense fall apart. Without good teamwork, they won’t survive. Its base stats are 42 HP, 52 Attack, 67 Defense, 39 Special Attack, 56 Special Defense, 50 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 306. It has the abilities Tough Claws and Sniper, with the hidden ability Pickpocket. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 31.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slash (level 13), Mud Slap (level 18), Clamp (level 20), Rock Polish (level 24), Ancient Power (level 28), Hone Claws (level 32). It belongs to the Water3 egg groups.

title: Barbaracle
text: Barbaracle is a Rock and Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Collective Pokémon. It evolves from Binacle. When they evolve, two Binacle multiply into seven. They fight with the power of seven Binacle. Barbaracle’s legs and hands have minds of their own, and they will move independently. But they usually follow the head’s orders. Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs. Having an eye on each palm allows it to keep watch in all directions. In a pinch, its limbs start to act on their own to ensure the enemy’s defeat. Its base stats are 72 HP, 105 Attack, 115 Defense, 54 Special Attack, 86 Special Defense, 68 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 500. It has the abilities Tough Claws and Sniper, with the hidden ability Pickpocket. It stands 1.3 meters tall and weighs 96.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Slash (level 13), Mud Slap (level 18), Clamp (level 20), Rock Polish (level 24), Ancient Power (level 28), Hone Claws (level 32). It belongs to the Water3 egg groups.

title: Skrelp
text: Skrelp is a Poison and Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Mock Kelp Pokémon. Camouflaged as rotten kelp, they spray liquid poison on prey that approaches unawares and then finish it off. It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution. It vanishes into seaweed and remains perfectly still to avoid attacks from large Pokémon. Rotten seaweed is its main food source. It’s not good at swimming, so it clings to the seaweed. In the Alola region, it’s often found near Dhelmise. It drifts in the ocean, blending in with floating seaweed. When other Pokémon come to feast on the seaweed, Skrelp feasts on them instead. Skrelp looks like a piece of rotten seaweed, so it can blend in with seaweed drifting on the ocean and avoid being detected by enemies. Its base stats are 50 HP, 60 Attack, 60 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 30 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 320. It has the abilities Poison Point and Poison Touch, with the hidden ability Adaptability. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 7.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Acid (level 15), Camouflage (level 19), Poison Tail (level 23), Water Pulse (level 25), Double Team (level 28), Toxic (level 32). It belongs to the Water1 and Dragon egg groups.

title: Dragalge
text: Dragalge is a Poison and Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Mock Kelp Pokémon. It evolves from Skrelp. Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory. Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return. This vicious Pokémon sprays a poisonous liquid on opponents that come near. For whatever reason, it gets along really well with Dhelmise. It blends in with seaweed to ambush its prey and then takes them down with a poisonous liquid strong enough to melt metal. Dragalge uses a poisonous liquid capable of corroding metal to send tankers that enter its territory to the bottom of the sea. Dragalge generates dragon energy by sticking the plume on its head out above the ocean’s surface and bathing it in sunlight. Its base stats are 65 HP, 75 Attack, 90 Defense, 97 Special Attack, 123 Special Defense, 44 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 494. It has the abilities Poison Point and Poison Touch, with the hidden ability Adaptability. It stands 1.8 meters tall and weighs 81.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Camouflage (level 19), Poison Tail (level 23), Water Pulse (level 25), Double Team (level 28), Toxic (level 32), Aqua Tail (level 35). It belongs to the Water1 and Dragon egg groups.

title: Clauncher
text: Clauncher is a Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Water Gun Pokémon. They knock down flying prey by firing compressed water from their massive claws like shooting a pistol. Through controlled explosions of internal gas, it can expel water like a pistol shot. At close distances, it can shatter rock. Its claws occasionally fall off, and it keeps a low profile until they grow back. The meat of its claws is so delicious! It moves around by jetting water from its right pincer. It has a poor sense of balance, so it’s terrible at swimming straight. Clauncher’s claws can fall off during battle, but they’ll regenerate. The meat inside the claws is popular as a delicacy in Galar. By detonating gas that accumulates in its right claw, this Pokémon launches water like a bullet. This is how Clauncher defeats its enemies. Its base stats are 50 HP, 53 Attack, 62 Defense, 58 Special Attack, 63 Special Defense, 44 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 330. It has the abilities Mega Launcher. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 8.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bubble Beam (level 20), Swords Dance (level 25), Hone Claws (level 25), Crabhammer (level 30), Water Pulse (level 34), Smack Down (level 39). It belongs to the Water1 and Water3 egg groups.

title: Clawitzer
text: Clawitzer is a Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Howitzer Pokémon. It evolves from Clauncher. Their enormous claws launch cannonballs of water powerful enough to pierce tanker hulls. By expelling water from the nozzle in the back of its claw, it can move at a speed of 60 knots. It compresses the water it has sucked up and then projects it with enough power to punch a hole right through a thick sheet of iron. Its right arm is packed with meat. When its pincer falls off, it’s exported to be used as a cooking ingredient. After using the feelers on its oversized claw to detect the location of prey, Clawitzer launches a cannonball of water at its target. Clawitzer’s right arm is a cannon that launches projectiles made of seawater. Shots from a Clawitzer’s cannon arm can sink a tanker. Its base stats are 71 HP, 73 Attack, 88 Defense, 120 Special Attack, 89 Special Defense, 59 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 500. It has the abilities Mega Launcher. It stands 1.3 meters tall and weighs 35.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Flail (level 16), Bubble Beam (level 20), Swords Dance (level 25), Hone Claws (level 25), Crabhammer (level 30), Water Pulse (level 34). It belongs to the Water1 and Water3 egg groups.

title: Helioptile
text: Helioptile is a Electric and Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Generator Pokémon. They make their home in deserts. They can generate their energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement. The frills on either side of its head have cells that generate electricity when exposed to sunlight. When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves. The sun powers this Pokémon’s electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness. Its base stats are 44 HP, 38 Attack, 33 Defense, 61 Special Attack, 43 Special Defense, 70 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 289. It has the abilities Dry Skin and Sand Veil, with the hidden ability Solar Power. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 6.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Mud Slap (level 13), Quick Attack (level 17), Razor Wind (level 22), Parabolic Charge (level 25), Thunder Wave (level 31), Bulldoze (level 35). It belongs to the Monster and Dragon egg groups.

title: Heliolisk
text: Heliolisk is a Electric and Normal type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Generator Pokémon. It evolves from Helioptile. They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper. It stimulates its muscles with electricity, boosting the strength in its legs and enabling it to run 100 yards in five seconds. A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them. One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city. Its base stats are 62 HP, 55 Attack, 52 Defense, 109 Special Attack, 94 Special Defense, 109 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 481. It has the abilities Dry Skin and Sand Veil, with the hidden ability Solar Power. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 21.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Thunder (level 1), Quick Attack (level 1), Mud Slap (level 1), Charge (level 1), Discharge (level 1), Volt Switch (level 1). It belongs to the Monster and Dragon egg groups.

title: Tyrunt
text: Tyrunt is a Rock and Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Royal Heir Pokémon. This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. If something happens that it doesn’t like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild. Its immense jaws have enough destructive force that it can chew up an automobile. It lived 100 million years ago. Its large jaw has incredible destructive power. Some theories suggest that its restored form is different from its form of long ago. Tyrunt is spoiled and selfish. It may just be trying to frolic, but sometimes the ones it’s trying to frolic with are gravely injured. This is an ancient Pokémon, revived in modern times. It has a violent disposition, and it’ll tear apart anything it gets between its hefty jaws. This Pokémon is selfish and likes to be pampered. It can also inflict grievous wounds on its Trainer just by playing around. Its base stats are 58 HP, 89 Attack, 77 Defense, 45 Special Attack, 45 Special Defense, 48 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 362. It has the abilities Strong Jaw, with the hidden ability Sturdy. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 26.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bite (level 17), Charm (level 20), Ancient Power (level 26), Rock Slide (level 28), Dragon Tail (level 30), Crunch (level 34). It belongs to the Monster and Dragon egg groups.

title: Tyrantrum
text: Tyrantrum is a Rock and Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Despot Pokémon. It evolves from Tyrunt. Thanks to its gargantuan jaws, which could shred thick metal plates as if they were paper, it was invincible in the ancient world it once inhabited. Nothing could stop this Pokémon 100 million years ago, so it behaved like a king. Complete restoration is impossible, allowing room for theories that its entire body was once covered in a feather-like coat. The king of the ancient world, it can easily crunch a car with the devastating strength of its enormous jaws. This Pokémon is from about 100,000,000 years ago. It has the presence of a king, vicious but magnificent. A single bite of Tyrantrum’s massive jaws will demolish a car. This Pokémon was the king of the ancient world. Its base stats are 82 HP, 121 Attack, 119 Defense, 69 Special Attack, 59 Special Defense, 71 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 521. It has the abilities Strong Jaw, with the hidden ability Rock Head. It stands 2.5 meters tall and weighs 270.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Charm (level 20), Ancient Power (level 26), Dragon Tail (level 30), Crunch (level 34), Dragon Claw (level 37), Thrash (level 42). It belongs to the Monster and Dragon egg groups.

title: Amaura
text: Amaura is a Rock and Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Tundra Pokémon. This ancient Pokémon was restored from part of its body that had been frozen in ice for over 100 million years. This calm Pokémon lived in a cold land where there were no violent predators like Tyrantrum. It lived in cold areas in ancient times. It’s said that when Amaura whinnies, auroras appear in the night sky. Amaura was restored successfully, but it’s not expected to live long because of the heat of the current environment. This Pokémon was successfully restored from a fossil. In the past, it lived with others of its kind in cold lands where there were fewer predators. Amaura is an ancient Pokémon that has gone extinct. Specimens of this species can sometimes be found frozen in ice. Its base stats are 77 HP, 59 Attack, 50 Defense, 67 Special Attack, 63 Special Defense, 46 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 362. It has the abilities Refrigerate, with the hidden ability Snow Warning. It stands 1.3 meters tall and weighs 25.2 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Round (level 30), Avalanche (level 34), Freeze Dry (level 36), Hail (level 38), Nature Power (level 41), Encore (level 44). It belongs to the Monster egg groups.

title: Aurorus
text: Aurorus is a Rock and Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Tundra Pokémon. It evolves from Amaura. The diamond-shaped crystals on its body expel air as cold as -240 degrees Fahrenheit, surrounding its enemies and encasing them in ice. Using its diamond-shaped crystals, it can instantly create a wall of ice to block an opponent’s attack. An Aurorus was found frozen solid within a glacier, just as it appeared long ago, which became quite a big event in the news. This usually quiet and kindly Pokémon has a surface temperature of around –240 degrees Fahrenheit. Aurorus was restored from a fossil. It’s said that when this Pokémon howls, auroras appear in the night sky. When gripped by rage, Aurorus will emanate freezing air, covering everything around it in ice. Its base stats are 123 HP, 77 Attack, 72 Defense, 99 Special Attack, 92 Special Defense, 58 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 521. It has the abilities Refrigerate, with the hidden ability Snow Warning. It stands 2.7 meters tall and weighs 225.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Ancient Power (level 26), Round (level 30), Avalanche (level 34), Hail (level 38), Nature Power (level 43), Encore (level 46). It belongs to the Monster egg groups.

title: Sylveon
text: Sylveon is a Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Intertwining Pokémon. It evolves from Eevee. It sends a soothing aura from its ribbonlike feelers to calm fights. It wraps its ribbonlike feelers around the arm of its beloved Trainer and walks with him or her. Its ribbonlike feelers give off an aura that weakens hostility in its prey, causing them to let down their guard. Then it attacks. When this Pokémon sights its prey, it swirls its ribbonlike feelers as a distraction. A moment later, it pounces. Once a fight breaks out, it will unflinchingly charge at dragon Pokémon that are many times larger than itself. Sylveon wraps its ribbonlike feelers around its Trainer’s arm because this touch enables it to read its Trainer’s feelings. Its base stats are 95 HP, 65 Attack, 65 Defense, 110 Special Attack, 130 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 525. It has the abilities Cute Charm, with the hidden ability Pixilate. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 23.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Draining Kiss (level 20), Mimic (level 25), Skill Swap (level 25), Play Rough (level 25), Misty Terrain (level 29), Light Screen (level 33). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Hawlucha
text: Hawlucha is a Fighting and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Wrestling Pokémon. Although its body is small, its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama. With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against. It overwhelms opponents with quick moves, but sometimes it showboats for too long when it’s using a special move and gets itself into a pinch. In combat, Hawlucha leaps nimbly about, taking advantage of its opponents’ blind spots. It’s also skilled at using superb submission holds. It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move. It always strikes a pose before going for its finishing move. Sometimes opponents take advantage of that time to counterattack. Its base stats are 78 HP, 92 Attack, 75 Defense, 74 Special Attack, 63 Special Defense, 118 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 500. It has the abilities Limber and Unburden, with the hidden ability Mold Breaker. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 21.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Fling (level 24), Flying Press (level 28), Taunt (level 32), Bounce (level 32), Endeavor (level 36), Feather Dance (level 40). It belongs to the Flying and Humanshape egg groups.

title: Dedenne
text: Dedenne is a Electric and Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Antenna Pokémon. Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances. It uses its tail to absorb electricity from power plants or from outlets in houses, and then it fires the electricity from its whiskers. It sneaks into people’s homes and steals electricity from their outlets. When your electric bill seems off, it’s the handiwork of this rascal. Its upper whiskers are sensors that survey its surroundings. Its lower whiskers are organs that shoot electricity. A Dedenne’s whiskers pick up electrical waves other Dedenne send out. These Pokémon share locations of food or electricity with one another. Since Dedenne can’t generate much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon. Its base stats are 67 HP, 58 Attack, 57 Defense, 81 Special Attack, 67 Special Defense, 101 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 431. It has the abilities Cheek Pouch and Pickup, with the hidden ability Plus. It stands 0.2 meters tall and weighs 2.2 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Thunder Wave (level 23), Volt Switch (level 26), Rest (level 30), Snore (level 31), Charge Beam (level 34), Entrainment (level 39). It belongs to the Ground and Fairy egg groups.

title: Carbink
text: Carbink is a Rock and Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Jewel Pokémon. Born from the temperatures and pressures deep underground, it fires beams from the stone in its head. It has slept underground for hundreds of millions of years since its birth. It’s occasionally found during the excavation of caves. Born from the high temperatures and pressures deep underground, it defends itself by firing beams from the jewel part of its body. Although this Pokémon is not especially rare, its glittering, jewel-draped body draws attention from people. Some say that deep beneath the surface of the world, a pack of Carbink live with their queen in a kingdom of jewels. To keep the jewels on their bodies free of tarnish, packs of Carbink use their soft manes to polish one another’s jewels. Its base stats are 50 HP, 50 Attack, 150 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 150 Special Defense, 50 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 500. It has the abilities Clear Body, with the hidden ability Sturdy. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 5.7 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Guard Split (level 27), Ancient Power (level 31), Rock Slide (level 35), Flail (level 35), Skill Swap (level 40), Power Gem (level 46). It belongs to the Fairy and Mineral egg groups.

title: Goomy
text: Goomy is a Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Soft Tissue Pokémon. The weakest Dragon-type Pokémon, it lives in damp, shady places, so its body doesn’t dry out. It’s covered in a slimy membrane that makes any punches or kicks slide off it harmlessly. The weakest of all Dragon-type Pokémon, it’s unable to breathe if its skin dries out, so it sticks to shady places. Its source of protection is its slimy, germ-laden mucous membrane. Anyone who touches it needs some thorough hand-washing. It uses its horns to check out its surroundings. They’re very sensitive, so if you grab them, it will feel a strong shock and be unable to move. Its body is mostly water. If it dries out, it will die, so it’s always sitting with perfect stillness in the shade. Its base stats are 45 HP, 50 Attack, 35 Defense, 55 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 40 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 300. It has the abilities Sap Sipper and Hydration, with the hidden ability Gooey. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 2.8 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Dragon Breath (level 18), Rain Dance (level 25), Water Pulse (level 25), Flail (level 28), Body Slam (level 32), Shelter (level 34). It belongs to the Dragon egg groups.

title: Sliggoo
text: Sliggoo is a Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Soft Tissue Pokémon. It evolves from Goomy. It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can’t see anything. Its four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose. It has trouble drawing a line between friends and food. It will calmly try to melt and eat even those it gets along well with. This Pokémon’s mucous can dissolve anything. Toothless, it sprays mucous on its prey. Once they’re nicely dissolved, it slurps them up. It crawls along sluggishly. The swirly protrusion on its back is filled with its brain and other organs. Its entire body is covered with gooey mucus that can dissolve anything. Be sure to put gloves on if you’re going to touch it. Its base stats are 68 HP, 75 Attack, 53 Defense, 83 Special Attack, 113 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 452. It has the abilities Sap Sipper and Hydration, with the hidden ability Gooey. It stands 0.8 meters tall and weighs 17.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Protect (level 9), Bide (level 13), Dragon Breath (level 18), Rain Dance (level 25), Water Pulse (level 25), Flail (level 28). It belongs to the Dragon egg groups.

title: Goodra
text: Goodra is a Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Dragon Pokémon. It evolves from Sliggoo. This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime. It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that’s the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers. An amazingly friendly Pokémon, but if left to itself, loneliness overcomes it, and it oozes gooey tears. It gets picked on because it’s meek. But then, whoever teased it gets to feel the full force of its horns and a good swatting from its thick tail. It’s very friendly toward people. If you grow close to it, Goodra will hug you with its sticky, slime-covered body. Don’t get mad. Underneath its slimy skin is a thick layer of fat, and the combination of the two makes enemies’ attacks slide right off. Its base stats are 90 HP, 100 Attack, 70 Defense, 110 Special Attack, 150 Special Defense, 80 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 600. It has the abilities Sap Sipper and Hydration, with the hidden ability Gooey. It stands 2.0 meters tall and weighs 150.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Dragon Breath (level 18), Rain Dance (level 25), Water Pulse (level 25), Flail (level 28), Body Slam (level 32), Muddy Water (level 38). It belongs to the Dragon egg groups.

title: Klefki
text: Klefki is a Steel and Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Key Ring Pokémon. These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them. It never lets go of a key that it likes, so people give it the keys to vaults and safes as a way to prevent crime. This even-tempered Pokémon has a habit of collecting keys. It will sneak into people’s homes to steal their keys. It inserts its horn into chinks in metal, absorbing metal ions. For some reason, it collects keys. Although it’s unclear why it collects keys, giving it a key makes Klefki very happy. However, it apparently only likes master keys. Long ago it lived in mines, but once the minerals that make up its diet became scarcer, Klefki began appearing in human settlements. Its base stats are 57 HP, 80 Attack, 91 Defense, 80 Special Attack, 87 Special Defense, 75 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 470. It has the abilities Prankster, with the hidden ability Magician. It stands 0.2 meters tall and weighs 3.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Foul Play (level 27), Torment (level 32), Mirror Shot (level 34), Imprison (level 36), Flash Cannon (level 36), Recycle (level 40). It belongs to the Mineral egg groups.

title: Phantump
text: Phantump is a Ghost and Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Stump Pokémon. These Pokémon are created when spirits possess rotten tree stumps. They prefer to live in abandoned forests. According to old tales, these Pokémon are stumps possessed by the spirits of children who died while lost in the forest. These Pokémon are stumps possessed by the spirits of children who died in the forest. Their cries sound like eerie screams. According to legend, medicine to cure any illness can be made by plucking the green leaves on its head, brewing them, and boiling down the liquid. By imitating the voice of a child, it causes people to get hopelessly lost deep in the forest. It’s trying to make friends with them. An unknown spirit came to lodge in a tree stump, creating this Pokémon. A cure-all can be made from an infusion of its green leaves. Its base stats are 43 HP, 70 Attack, 48 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, 38 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 309. It has the abilities Natural Cure and Frisk, with the hidden ability Harvest. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 7.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Feint Attack (level 19), Hex (level 20), Leech Seed (level 23), Curse (level 28), Will O Wisp (level 31), Forests Curse (level 35). It belongs to the Plant and Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Trevenant
text: Trevenant is a Ghost and Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Elder Tree Pokémon. It evolves from Phantump. It can control trees at will. It will trap people who harm the forest, so they can never leave. Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It’s kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body. This Pokémon is said to devour anyone daring to ravage the forest. To the creatures dwelling in the forest, it offers great kindness. Through its roots, it exerts control over other trees. A deadly curse falls upon anyone cutting down trees in forests where Trevenant dwell. It’s feared as a ghost of the forest. Lumberjacks bring along Fire types, which Trevenant hates, when they enter the forest. This Pokémon controls trees via roots stretching from its feet, and it will attack anyone that dares to ravage the forest. Its base stats are 85 HP, 110 Attack, 76 Defense, 65 Special Attack, 82 Special Defense, 56 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 474. It has the abilities Natural Cure and Frisk, with the hidden ability Harvest. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 71.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Feint Attack (level 19), Hex (level 20), Leech Seed (level 23), Curse (level 28), Will O Wisp (level 31), Forests Curse (level 35). It belongs to the Plant and Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Pumpkaboo average
text: Pumpkaboo average is a Ghost and Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Pumpkin Pokémon. The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active. It is said to carry wandering spirits to the place where they belong so they can move on. Spirits that wander this world are placed into Pumpkaboo’s body. They’re then moved on to the afterlife. The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it. Its base stats are 49 HP, 66 Attack, 70 Defense, 44 Special Attack, 55 Special Defense, 51 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 335. It has the abilities Pickup and Frisk, with the hidden ability Insomnia. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 5.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Scary Face (level 4), Trick Or Treat (level 6), Worry Seed (level 11), Razor Leaf (level 16), Leech Seed (level 20), Bullet Seed (level 26). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Gourgeist average
text: Gourgeist average is a Ghost and Grass type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Pumpkin Pokémon. It evolves from Pumpkaboo. Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed. It enwraps its prey in its hairlike arms. It sings joyfully as it observes the suffering of its prey. Eerie cries emanate from its body in the dead of night. The sounds are said to be the wails of spirits who are suffering in the afterlife. In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife. Its base stats are 65 HP, 90 Attack, 122 Defense, 58 Special Attack, 75 Special Defense, 84 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 494. It has the abilities Pickup and Frisk, with the hidden ability Insomnia. It stands 0.9 meters tall and weighs 12.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Scary Face (level 4), Trick Or Treat (level 6), Worry Seed (level 11), Razor Leaf (level 16), Leech Seed (level 20), Bullet Seed (level 26). It belongs to the Indeterminate egg groups.

title: Bergmite
text: Bergmite is a Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Ice Chunk Pokémon. It blocks opponents’ attacks with the ice that shields its body. It uses cold air to repair any cracks with new ice. Using air of -150 degrees Fahrenheit, they freeze opponents solid. They live in herds above the snow line on mountains. They chill the air around them to −150 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing the water in the air into ice that they use as armor. This Pokémon lives in areas of frigid cold. It secures itself to the back of an Avalugg by freezing its feet in place. Lives on mountains blanketed in perennial snow. It freezes water vapor in the air to make the ice helmet that it dons for defense. Its base stats are 55 HP, 69 Attack, 85 Defense, 32 Special Attack, 35 Special Defense, 28 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 304. It has the abilities Own Tempo and Ice Body, with the hidden ability Sturdy. It stands 1.0 meters tall and weighs 99.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Curse (level 22), Ice Fang (level 26), Iron Defense (level 27), Ice Ball (level 30), Crunch (level 33), Rapid Spin (level 35). It belongs to the Monster and Mineral egg groups.

title: Avalugg
text: Avalugg is a Ice type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Iceberg Pokémon. It evolves from Bergmite. Its ice-covered body is as hard as steel. Its cumbersome frame crushes anything that stands in its way. The way several Bergmite huddle on its back makes it look like an aircraft carrier made of ice. At high latitudes, this Pokémon can be found with clusters of Bergmite on its back as it swims among the icebergs. As Avalugg moves about during the day, the cracks in its body deepen. The Pokémon’s body returns to a pristine state overnight. The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow. Its base stats are 95 HP, 117 Attack, 184 Defense, 44 Special Attack, 46 Special Defense, 28 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 514. It has the abilities Own Tempo and Ice Body, with the hidden ability Sturdy. It stands 2.0 meters tall and weighs 505.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Sharpen (level 20), Curse (level 22), Ice Fang (level 26), Ice Ball (level 30), Rapid Spin (level 35), Avalanche (level 42). It belongs to the Monster and Mineral egg groups.

title: Noibat
text: Noibat is a Flying and Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Sound Wave Pokémon. They live in pitch-black caves. Their enormous ears can emit ultrasonic waves of 200,000 hertz. Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves. Fruits are its favorite foods. This gourmet carefully picks out just the ripe ones using its sonar. It flies around in search of fruit to eat. It uses ultrasonic waves to detect which fruits are ripe. After nightfall, they emerge from the caves they nest in during the day. Using their ultrasonic waves, they go on the hunt for ripened fruit. No wavelength of sound is beyond Noibat’s ability to produce. The ultrasonic waves it generates can overcome much larger Pokémon. Its base stats are 40 HP, 30 Attack, 35 Defense, 45 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 55 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 245. It has the abilities Frisk and Infiltrator, with the hidden ability Telepathy. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 8.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Wing Attack (level 16), Agility (level 18), Air Cutter (level 23), Roost (level 27), Razor Wind (level 31), Tailwind (level 35). It belongs to the Flying and Dragon egg groups.

title: Noivern
text: Noivern is a Flying and Dragon type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Sound Wave Pokémon. It evolves from Noibat. They fly around on moonless nights and attack careless prey. Nothing can beat them in a battle in the dark. The ultrasonic waves it emits from its ears can reduce a large boulder to pebbles. It swoops out of the dark to attack. Although it has a violent disposition, if you give it a nice ripe fruit that it loves, Noivern will suddenly become tame. This hot-blooded Pokémon attacks anything it sees with ultrasonic waves that can crush boulders. Aggressive and cruel, this Pokémon will ruthlessly torment enemies that are helpless in the dark. Flying through the darkness, it weakens enemies with ultrasonic waves that could crush stone. Its fangs finish the fight. Its base stats are 85 HP, 70 Attack, 80 Defense, 97 Special Attack, 80 Special Defense, 123 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 535. It has the abilities Frisk and Infiltrator, with the hidden ability Telepathy. It stands 1.5 meters tall and weighs 85.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Bite (level 13), Wing Attack (level 16), Agility (level 18), Air Cutter (level 23), Roost (level 27), Razor Wind (level 31). It belongs to the Flying and Dragon egg groups.

title: Xerneas
text: Xerneas is a Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Life Pokémon. Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival. When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life. Its base stats are 126 HP, 131 Attack, 95 Defense, 131 Special Attack, 98 Special Defense, 99 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 680. It has the abilities Fairy Aura. It stands 3.0 meters tall and weighs 215.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Moonblast (level 35), Megahorn (level 44), Night Slash (level 51), Horn Leech (level 55), Psych Up (level 59), Misty Terrain (level 63). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Yveltal
text: Yveltal is a Dark and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Destruction Pokémon. When this legendary Pokémon’s wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of living creatures. When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more. Its base stats are 126 HP, 131 Attack, 95 Defense, 131 Special Attack, 98 Special Defense, 99 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 680. It has the abilities Dark Aura. It stands 5.8 meters tall and weighs 203.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Disable (level 35), Dark Pulse (level 44), Foul Play (level 51), Phantom Force (level 55), Psychic (level 59), Dragon Rush (level 63). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Zygarde 50
text: Zygarde 50 is a Dragon and Ground type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Order Pokémon. When the Kalos region’s ecosystem falls into disarray, it appears and reveals its secret power. It’s hypothesized that it’s monitoring those who destroy the ecosystem from deep in the cave where it lives. This is Zygarde’s form when it has gathered 50% of its cells. It wipes out all those who oppose it, showing not a shred of mercy. It’s thought to be monitoring the ecosystem. There are rumors that even greater power lies hidden within it. This is Zygarde’s form when about half of its pieces have been assembled. It plays the role of monitoring the ecosystem. Some say it can change to an even more powerful form when battling those who threaten the ecosystem. Its base stats are 108 HP, 100 Attack, 121 Defense, 81 Special Attack, 95 Special Defense, 95 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 600. It has the abilities Aura Break. It stands 5.0 meters tall and weighs 305.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Sandstorm (level 35), Haze (level 44), Crunch (level 51), Earthquake (level 55), Camouflage (level 59), Dragon Pulse (level 63). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Diancie
text: Diancie is a Rock and Fairy type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Jewel Pokémon. A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world. It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands. Its base stats are 50 HP, 100 Attack, 150 Defense, 100 Special Attack, 150 Special Defense, 50 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 600. It has the abilities Clear Body. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 8.8 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Rock Polish (level 35), Skill Swap (level 40), Power Gem (level 40), Trick Room (level 46), Rock Slide (level 49), Stone Edge (level 49). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Hoopa
text: Hoopa is a Psychic and Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Mischief Pokémon. This troublemaker sends anything and everything to faraway places using its loop, which can warp space. It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place. In its true form, it possess a huge amount of power. Legends of its avarice tell how it once carried off an entire castle to gain the treasure hidden within. It is said to be able to seize anything it desires with its six rings and six huge arms. With its power sealed, it is transformed into a much smaller form. Its base stats are 80 HP, 110 Attack, 60 Defense, 150 Special Attack, 130 Special Defense, 70 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 600. It has the abilities Magician. It stands 0.5 meters tall and weighs 9.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Skill Swap (level 25), Power Split (level 29), Guard Split (level 35), Zen Headbutt (level 46), Phantom Force (level 46), Wonder Room (level 50). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Volcanion
text: Volcanion is a Fire and Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 6. It is known as the Steam Pokémon. It lets out billows of steam and disappears into the dense fog. It’s said to live in mountains where humans do not tread. It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain. Its base stats are 80 HP, 110 Attack, 120 Defense, 130 Special Attack, 90 Special Defense, 70 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 600. It has the abilities Water Absorb. It stands 1.7 meters tall and weighs 195.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Water Pulse (level 21), Stomp (level 28), Scary Face (level 30), Scald (level 32), Incinerate (level 36), Weather Ball (level 40). It belongs to the No Eggs egg groups.

title: Rowlet
text: Rowlet is a Grass and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 7. It is known as the Grass Quill Pokémon. This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night. Silently it glides, drawing near its targets. Before they even notice it, it begins to pelt them with vicious kicks. It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable. It feels relaxed in tight, dark places and has been known to use its Trainer’s pocket or bag as a nest. At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks. During the day, it builds up energy via photosynthesis. At night, it flies silently through the sky, on the prowl for prey. Its base stats are 68 HP, 55 Attack, 55 Defense, 50 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 42 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 320. It has the abilities Overgrow, with the hidden ability Long Reach. It stands 0.3 meters tall and weighs 1.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Pluck (level 22), Synthesis (level 25), Air Slash (level 25), Fury Attack (level 29), Sucker Punch (level 32), Leaf Blade (level 36). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Dartrix
text: Dartrix is a Grass and Flying type Pokemon introduced in Generation 7. It is known as the Blade Quill Pokémon. It evolves from Rowlet. A bit of a dandy, it spends its free time preening its wings. Its preoccupation with any dirt on its plumage can leave it unable to battle. It throws sharp feathers called blade quills at enemies or prey. It seldom misses. This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don’t groom it diligently, it will stop listening to you. Supremely sensitive to the presence of others, it can detect opponents standing behind it, flinging its sharp feathers to take them out. It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills. It never slacks when it comes to the task of cleaning its feathers. Thorough preening keeps it looking spiffy and its blade quills nice and sharp. Its base stats are 78 HP, 75 Attack, 75 Defense, 70 Special Attack, 70 Special Defense, 52 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 420. It has the abilities Overgrow, with the hidden ability Long Reach. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 16.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Pluck (level 24), Air Slash (level 25), Synthesis (level 28), Fury Attack (level 33), Sucker Punch (level 37), Leaf Blade (level 42). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Decidueye
text: Decidueye is a Grass and Ghost type Pokemon introduced in Generation 7. It is known as the Arrow Quill Pokémon. It evolves from Dartrix. It fires arrow quills from its wings with such precision, they can pierce a pebble at distances over a hundred yards. Although basically cool and cautious, when it’s caught by surprise, it’s seized by panic. It nocks its arrow quills and shoots them at opponents. When it simply can’t afford to miss, it tugs the vine on its head to improve its focus. Decidueye can nock and fire an arrow at an enemy in a tenth of a second, so its battles are decided in the blink of an eye. As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye’s shots never miss. In a tenth of a second, it can nock and fire an arrow quill, piercing an opponent’s weak point before they notice what’s happening. Its base stats are 78 HP, 107 Attack, 75 Defense, 100 Special Attack, 100 Special Defense, 70 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 530. It has the abilities Overgrow, with the hidden ability Long Reach. It stands 1.6 meters tall and weighs 36.6 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Ominous Wind (level 16), Foresight (level 19), Pluck (level 24), Synthesis (level 28), Fury Attack (level 33), Sucker Punch (level 38). It belongs to the Flying egg groups.

title: Litten
text: Litten is a Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 7. It is known as the Fire Cat Pokémon. While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs. It doesn’t allow its emotions to be easily seen. Earning its trust takes time. It prefers solitude. If you try too hard to get close to it, it won’t open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no. Its coat regrows twice a year. When the time comes, Litten sets its own body on fire and burns away the old fur. It spends even the smallest amount of downtime grooming its fur with its tongue. Loose fur gathers in its stomach and serves as fuel for fiery moves. Trying to pet Litten before it trusts you will result in a nasty scratch from its sharp claws. Be careful. Its base stats are 45 HP, 65 Attack, 40 Defense, 60 Special Attack, 40 Special Defense, 70 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 320. It has the abilities Blaze, with the hidden ability Intimidate. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 4.3 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Double Kick (level 16), Roar (level 18), Bite (level 22), Swagger (level 25), Fury Swipes (level 29), Thrash (level 32). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Torracat
text: Torracat is a Fire type Pokemon introduced in Generation 7. It is known as the Fire Cat Pokémon. It evolves from Litten. At its throat, it bears a bell of fire. The bell rings brightly whenever this Pokémon spits fire. It boasts powerful front legs. With a single punch, it can bend an iron bar right over. It can act spoiled if it grows close to its Trainer. A powerful Pokémon, its sharp claws can leave its Trainer’s whole body covered in scratches. When its mane is standing on end, you can tell it’s feeling good. When it isn’t feeling well, its fur will lie down flat. In the midst of battle, the fire pouch on Torracat’s neck rings like a bell and produces stronger flames than usual. When facing a powerful enemy, Torracat’s fighting spirit gets pumped up, and its fire bell blazes hotter. Its base stats are 65 HP, 85 Attack, 50 Defense, 80 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, 90 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 420. It has the abilities Blaze, with the hidden ability Intimidate. It stands 0.7 meters tall and weighs 25.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Double Kick (level 16), Roar (level 19), Bite (level 24), Swagger (level 28), Fury Swipes (level 33), Thrash (level 37). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Incineroar
text: Incineroar is a Fire and Dark type Pokemon introduced in Generation 7. It is known as the Heel Pokémon. It evolves from Torracat. This Pokémon has a violent, selfish disposition. If it’s not in the mood to listen, it will ignore its Trainer’s orders with complete nonchalance. After hurling ferocious punches and flinging furious kicks, it finishes opponents off by spewing fire from around its navel. Although it’s rough mannered and egotistical, it finds beating down unworthy opponents boring. It gets motivated for stronger opponents. When its fighting spirit is set alight, the flames around its waist become especially intense. It excels at violent, no-holds-barred battles. The temperature of the flames that issue from its navel exceeds 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit. Incineroar’s rough and aggressive behavior is its most notable trait, but the way it helps out small Pokémon shows that it has a kind side as well. Its base stats are 95 HP, 115 Attack, 90 Defense, 80 Special Attack, 90 Special Defense, 60 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 530. It has the abilities Blaze, with the hidden ability Intimidate. It stands 1.8 meters tall and weighs 83.0 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Roar (level 19), Bite (level 24), Swagger (level 28), Fury Swipes (level 33), Thrash (level 38), Flamethrower (level 44). It belongs to the Ground egg groups.

title: Popplio
text: Popplio is a Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 7. It is known as the Sea Lion Pokémon. This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It’s famous for being a hard worker. This Pokémon can control water bubbles. It practices diligently so it can learn to make big bubbles. The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day. Popplio gets on top of its bouncy water balloons to jump higher. It’s quite the acrobatic fighter! It creates special bouncy balloons to send at its opponents. When the balloons pop, the sheer force of their bursting is what deals damage. If Popplio want to create big, powerful balloons, they must be persistent. It takes daily practice for these Pokémon to develop their skills. Its base stats are 50 HP, 54 Attack, 54 Defense, 66 Special Attack, 56 Special Defense, 40 Speed, giving it a base stat total of 320. It has the abilities Torrent, with the hidden ability Liquid Voice. It stands 0.4 meters tall and weighs 7.5 kilograms. Some of its notable moves include Icy Wind (level 16), Encore (level 18), Bubble Beam (level 22), Sing (level 25), Double Slap (level 29), Hyper Voice (level 32). It belongs to the Water1 and Ground egg groups.

title: Brionne
text: Brionne is a Water type Pokemon introduced in Generation 7. It is known as the Pop Star Pokémon. It evolves from Popplio. A skillful dancer, it creates a sequence of water balloons as it dances, and briskly bombards its enemies. It cares deeply for its companions. When its Trainer is feeling down, it performs a cheery dance to try and help. It gets excited when it sees a dance it doesn’t know. This hard worker practices diligently until it can learn that dance. It attacks by smacking its enemies with the exploding water balloons that it creates. As if dancing, it artfully dodges the attacks of its enemies. All the while, it’s busy forming a bunch of balloons to overwhelm its foes. On nights when the sea is calm, Brionne dance with one another to the singing of the Primarina that’s leading them.
